entries.translation: I am pleased with Allah as a Lord, and Islam as a religion and Muhammad (saws) as a Prophet. [Three times]
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #87

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #88
entries.arabic: ((يَا حَيُّ يَا قَيُّومُ بِرَحْمَتِكَ أَسْتَغيثُ أَصْلِحْ لِي شَأْنِيَ كُلَّهُ وَلاَ تَكِلْنِي إِلَى نَفْسِي طَرْفَةَ عَيْنٍ)).
entries.transliteration: Yâhayyû, yâ qayyûmu bi rahmatika astaghîth(u).Aslihlî sha'nî kullah(u)wa lâ takilnî ilâ nafsîtarfatacayn(in)
entries.translation: O Ever Living, O Self-Subsisting and Supporter of all, by Your mercy I seek assistance, rectify for me all of my affairs and do not leave me to myself, even for the blink of an eye.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #88

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #89
entries.arabic: ((أَصْبَحْنَا وَأَصْبَحَ الْمُلْكُ لِلَّهِ رَبِّ الْعَالَمِينَ، اللَّهُـمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ خَيْرَ هَذَا الْيَوْمِ:فَتْحَهُ، وَنَصْرَهُ، وَنورَهُ، وَبَرَكَتَهُ، وَهُدَاهُ، وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا فِيهِ وَشَرِّ مَا بَعْدَهُ)). [وإذا أمسى قال: أمسينا وأمسى الملك للَّه ربّ العالمين اللَّهم إني أسألك خير هذه الليلة: فتحها، ونصرها، ونورها، وبركتها، وهداها، وأعوذ بك من شر ما فيها، وشر ما بعدها.]
entries.transliteration: Asbahnâ wa asbaha-l-mulku li-l-lâhi rabbi-l-câlamîn. Allahumma innî as'alukakhayra hâdhâ-l-yawmi: fathahu, wa nusrahu, wa nûrahu, wa barakatahu,wa hudâhu. Wa acûdhu bika min sharri mâ fîhi wa sharri mâ bacdahu.
entries.translation: [In the morning :] We have reached the morning and at this very time all sovereignty belongs to Allah, Lord of the worlds. O Allah, I ask You for the good of this day, its triumphs and its victories, its light and its blessings and its guidance, and I take refuge in You from the evil of this day and the evil that follows it. / [In the evening :] We have reached the evening and at this very time all sovereignty belongs to Allah, Lord of the worlds. O Allah, I ask You for the good of tonight, its triumphs and its victories, its light and its blessings and its guidance, and I take refuge in You from the evil of tonight and the evil that follows it.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #89

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #90
entries.arabic: ((أَصْبَحْنا عَلَى فِطْرَةِ الْإِسْلاَمِ، وَعَلَى كَلِمَةِ الْإِخْلاَصِ، وَعَلَى دِينِ نَبِيِّنَا مُحَمَّدٍ صلى الله عليه وسلم، وَعَلَى مِلَّةِ أَبِينَا إِبْرَاهِيمَ، حَنِيفاً مُسْلِماً وَمَا كَانَ مِنَ الْمُشرِكِينَ)). [وإذا أمسى قال: أمسينا على فطرة الإسلام...]
entries.transliteration: Asbahnacalâ fitrati-l-islâmi, wacalâ kalimati-l-ikhlâsi, wacalâ nabiyyinâ Muhammadin,wacalâ millati abîna Ibrâhîma, hanîfan, musliman, wa mâ kâna mina-l-mushrikîn.
entries.translation: We rise upon the fitrah of Islam, and the word of pure faith, and upon the religion of our Prophet Muhammad (saws) and the religion of our forefather Ibraheem, who was a Muslim and of true faith and was not of those who associate others with Allah.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #90

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #91
entries.arabic: ((سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ وَبِحَمْدِهِ)) (مائة مرَّةٍ).
entries.transliteration: Subhâna l-lâhi wa bi-hamdihi.
entries.translation: How perfect Allah is and I praise Him. [one hundred times]
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #91

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #92
entries.arabic: ((لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لاَ شَرِيكَ لَهُ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ، وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ)) (عشرَ مرَّات) ، أَوْ (مرَّةً واحدةً عندَ الكَسَلِ).
entries.transliteration: Lâ ilâha illa l-lâhu wahdahu lâ sharîka lah(u)lahu-l-mulku walahu-l-hamd(u),wa huwacalâ kulli shay'in qadîr.
entries.translation: None has the right to be worshipped except Allah, alone, without partner, to Him belongs all sovereignty and praise and He is over all things omnipotent. [ten times, or just once]
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #92

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #93
entries.arabic: ((لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ، وَحْدَهُ لاَ شَرِيكَ لَهُ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ)) (مائةَ مرَّةٍ إذا أصبحَ).
entries.transliteration: Lâ ilâha illa l-lâhu wahdahu lâ sharîka lah(u)lahu-l-mulku walahu-l-hamd(u),wa huwacalâ kulli shay'in qadîr.
entries.translation: None has the right to be worshipped except Allah, alone, without partner, to Him belongs all sovereignty and praise, and He is over all things omnipotent. [one hundred times every day]
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #93

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #94
entries.arabic: ((سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ وَبِحَمْدِهِ: عَدَدَ خَلْقِهِ، وَرِضَا نَفْسِهِ، وَزِنَةَ عَرْشِهِ، وَمِدَادَ كَلِمَاتِهِ)) (ثلاثَ مرَّاتٍ إذا أصبحَ).
entries.transliteration: Subhâna l-lâhi wa bi-hamdihi,cadada khaqihi, wa ridâ nafsihi,wa zinatacarshihi, wa midâda kalimâtihi.
entries.translation: How perfect Allah is and I praise Him by the number of His creation and His pleasure, and by the weight of His throne, and the ink of His words. [three times]
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #94

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #95
entries.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ عِلْماً نَافِعاً، وَرِزْقاً طَيِّباً، وَعَمَلاً مُتَقَبَّلاً)) (إذا أصبحَ).
entries.transliteration: Allahumma innî as'alukacilman nâfican, wa rizqantayyiban,wacamalan mutaqabbalan.
entries.translation: O Allah, I ask You for knowledge which is beneficial and sustenance which is good, and deeds which are acceptable. (To be said after giving salam for the fajr prayer)
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #95

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #96
entries.arabic: ((أَسْتَغْفِرُ اللَّهَ وَأَتُوبُ إِلَيْهِ)) (مِائَةَ مَرَّةٍ فِي الْيَوْمِ).
entries.transliteration: Astaghfiru l-lâha wa atûbu ilayh(i).
entries.translation: I seek Your forgiveness, Allah, and repent unto You.'
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #96

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #97
entries.arabic: ((أَعُوذُ بِكَلِمَاتِ اللَّهِ التَّامَّاتِ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ)) (ثلاثَ مرَّاتٍ إذا أمسى).
entries.transliteration: Acûdhu bi-kalimati l-lâhi-t-tââmmâti min sharri mâ khalaq .
entries.translation: I take refuge in Allah's perfect words from the evil He has created. [Three times in the evening]
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #97

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #98
entries.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ صَلِّ وَسَلِّمْ عَلَى نَبَيِّنَا مُحَمَّدٍ)) (عشرَ مرَّاتٍ).
entries.transliteration: Allahummasalli wa sallimcalâ nabiyyinâ Muhammad.
entries.translation: O Allah, send prayers and blessings upon our prophet Muhammad. (ten times)
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #98

categories.name: Before sleeping
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 13 invocations.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #99
entries.arabic: ((يَجْمَعُ كَفَّيْهِ ثُمَّ يَنْفُثُ فِيهِمَا فَيَقْرَأُ فِيهِمَا:بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم ﴿قُلْ هُوَ اللَّهُ أَحَدٌ * اللَّهُ الصَّمَدُ* لَمْ يَلِدْ وَلَمْ يُولَدْ* وَلَمْ يَكُن لَّهُ كُفُواً أَحَدٌ﴾. بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم ﴿قُلْ أَعُوذُ بِرَبِّ الْفَلَقِ* مِن شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ* وَمِن شَرِّ غَاسِقٍ إِذَا وَقَبَ* وَمِن شَرِّ النَّفَّاثَاتِ فِي الْعُقَدِ* وَمِن شَرِّ حَاسِدٍ إِذَا حَسَدَ﴾. بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم ﴿قُلْ أَعُوذُ بِرَبِّ النَّاسِ* مَلِكِ النَّاسِ* إِلَهِ النَّاسِ* مِن شَرِّ الْوَسْوَاسِ الْخَنَّاسِ* الَّذِي يُوَسْوِسُ فِي صُدُورِ النَّاسِ* مِنَ الْجِنَّةِ وَ النَّاسِ﴾ ثُمَّ يَمْسَحُ بِهِمَا مَا اسْتَطَاعَ مِنْ جَسَدِهِ يَبْدَأُ بِهِمَا عَلَى رَأْسِهِ وَوَجْهِهِ وَمَا أَقبَلَ مِنْ جَسَدِهِ)) (يفعلُ ذلك ثلاثَ مرَّاتٍ).
entries.transliteration: Yajma'u kaffayhi thumma yanfuthu fihima, fa-yaqra'u fihima: Qul huwa Allahu ahad Allahu assamad Lam yalid walam yoolad Walam yakun lahu kufuwan ahad Qul aAAoothu birabbi alfalaq Min sharri ma khalaq Wamin sharri ghasiqin ithawaqab Wamin sharri annaffathatifee alAAuqad Wamin sharri hasidin itha hasad Qul aAAoothu birabbi annas Maliki annas Ilahi annas Min sharri alwaswasi alkhannas Allathee yuwaswisu fee sudoori annas Mina aljinnati wannas
entries.translation: Cup both hands, lightly blow into them, recite Surah Al-Ikhlas, Surah Al-Falaq and Surah An-Nas, then wipe over as much of the body as possible, beginning with the head and face. Say, "He is Allāh, [who is] One, Allāh, the Eternal Refuge. He neither begets nor is born, Nor is there to Him any equivalent." Say, "I seek refuge in the Lord of daybreak From the evil of that which He created And from the evil of darkness when it settles And from the evil of the blowers in knots And from the evil of an envier when he envies." Say, "I seek refuge in the Lord of mankind, The Sovereign of mankind, The God of mankind, From the evil of the retreating whisperer - Who whispers [evil] into the breasts of mankind - From among the jinn and mankind".
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #99 · Quran 112, 113 and 114; Al-Bukhari

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #100
entries.arabic: ﴿اللَّهُ لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ هُوَ الْحَيُّ الْقَيُّومُ لاَ تَأْخُذُهُ سِنَةٌ وَلاَ نَوْمٌ لَّهُ مَا فِي السَّمَوَاتِ وَمَا فِي الأَرْضِ مَن ذَا الَّذِي يَشْفَعُ عِنْـــــــــــدَهُ إِلاَّ بِإِذْنِهِ يَعْلَمُ مَا بَيْنَ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَمَا خَلْفَهُمْ وَلاَ يُحِيطُونَ بِشَيْءٍ مِّنْ عِلْمِهِ إِلاَّ بِمَا شَاء وَسِعَ كُرْسِيُّهُ السَّمَوَاتِ وَالأَرْضَ وَلاَ يَؤُودُهُ حِفْظُهُمَا وَهُوَ الْعَلِيُّ الْعَظِيمُ﴾.
entries.transliteration: Allahu la ilaha illahuwa alhayyu alqayyoomu la ta/khuthuhusinatun wala nawmun lahu ma fee assamawatiwama fee al-ardi man tha allatheeyashfaAAu AAindahu illa bi-ithnihi yaAAlamu mabayna aydeehim wama khalfahum wala yuheetoonabishay-in min AAilmihi illa bima shaawasiAAa kursiyyuhu assamawati wal-ardawala yaooduhu hifthuhumawahuwa alAAaliyyu alAAatheem
entries.translation: Allāh - there is no deity except Him, the Ever-Living, the Self-Sustaining. Neither drowsiness overtakes Him nor sleep. To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. Who is it that can intercede with Him except by His permission? He knows what is [presently] before them and what will be after them, and they encompass not a thing of His knowledge except for what He wills. His Kursī extends over the heavens and the earth, and their preservation tires Him not. And He is the Most High, the Most Great.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #100 · Quran 2:255

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #101
entries.arabic: ﴿آمَنَ الرَّسُولُ بِمَا أُنزِلَ إِلَيْهِ مِن رَّبِّهِ وَالْمُؤْمِنُونَ كُلٌّ آمَنَ بِاللَّهِ وَمَلآئِكَتِهِ وَكُتُبِهِ وَرُسُلِهِ لاَ نُفَرِّقُ بَيْنَ أَحَدٍ مِّن رُّسُلِهِ وَقَالُواْ سَمِعْنَا وَأَطَعْنَا غُفْرَانَكَ رَبَّنَا وَإِلَيْكَ الْمَصِيرُ* لاَ يُكَلِّفُ اللَّهُ نَفْساً إِلاَّ وُسْعَهَا لَهَا مَا كَسَبَتْ وَعَلَيْهَا مَا اكْتَسَبَتْ رَبَّنَا لاَ تُؤَاخِذْنَا إِن نَّسِينَا أَوْ أَخْطَأْنَا رَبَّنَا وَلاَ تَحْمِلْ عَلَيْنَا إِصْراً كَمَا حَمَلْتَهُ عَلَى الَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِنَا رَبَّنَا وَلاَ تُحَمِّلْنَا مَا لاَ طَاقَةَ لَنَا بِهِ وَاعْفُ عَنَّا وَاغْفِرْ لَنَا وَارْحَمْنَآ أَنتَ مَوْلاَنَا فَانصُرْنَا عَلَى الْقَوْمِ الْكَافِرِينَ﴾.
entries.transliteration: Amana arrasoolu bimaonzila ilayhi min rabbihi walmu/minoona kullun amanabillahi wamala-ikatihi wakutubihiwarusulihi la nufarriqu bayna ahadin min rusulihiwaqaloo samiAAna waataAAna ghufranakarabbana wa-ilayka almaseer La yukallifu Allahu nafsanilla wusAAaha laha ma kasabatwaAAalayha ma iktasabat rabbana latu-akhithna in naseena aw akhta/narabbana wala tahmil AAalayna isrankama hamaltahu AAala allatheena minqablina rabbana wala tuhammilnama la taqata lana bihi waAAfuAAanna waghfir lana warhamnaanta mawlana fansurna AAalaalqawmi alkafireen
entries.translation: The Messenger has believed in what was revealed to him from his Lord, and [so have] the believers. All of them have believed in Allāh and His angels and His books and His messengers, [saying], "We make no distinction between any of His messengers." And they say, "We hear and we obey. [We seek] Your forgiveness, our Lord, and to You is the [final] destination." Allāh does not charge a soul except [with that within] its capacity. It will have [the consequence of] what [good] it has gained, and it will bear [the consequence of] what [evil] it has earned. "Our Lord, do not impose blame upon us if we have forgotten or erred. Our Lord, and lay not upon us a burden like that which You laid upon those before us. Our Lord, and burden us not with that which we have no ability to bear. And pardon us; and forgive us; and have mercy upon us. You are our protector, so give us victory over the disbelieving people."
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #101 · Quran 2:285-286

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #102
entries.arabic: ((بِاسْمِكَ رَبِّي وَضَعْتُ جَنْبِي، وَبِكَ أَرْفَعُهُ، فَإِن أَمْسَكْتَ نَفْسِي فارْحَمْهَا، وَإِنْ أَرْسَلْتَهَا فَاحْفَظْهَا، بِمَا تَحْفَظُ بِهِ عِبَادَكَ الصَّالِحِينَ)).
entries.transliteration: Bismika rabbî wadactu janbî wa bika arfacuhu. Fa-in amsakta nafsî fa-rhamhâ, wa in arsaltahâ fa-hfazhâ bi-mâ tahfazu bihicibâdakas-sâlihîn.
entries.translation: In Your name my Lord, I lie down and in Your name I rise, so if You should take my soul then have mercy upon it, and if You should return my soul then protect it in the manner You do so with Your righteous servants.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #102

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #103
entries.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ إِنَّكَ خَلَقْتَ نَفْسِي وَأَنْتَ تَوَفَّاهَا، لَكَ مَمَاتُهَا وَمَحْياهَا، إِنْ أَحْيَيْتَهَا فَاحْفَظْهَا، وَإِنْ أَمَتَّهَا فَاغْفِرْ لَهَا. اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ العَافِيَةَ)).
entries.transliteration: Allâhumma innaka khalaqra nafsî wa anta tawaffâhâ. Laka mamâtuhâ wa mahyâhâ. In ahyaytahâ fa-hfazha, wa in amattahâ fa--ghfirlahâ. Allâhumma inni as'aluka-l-câfiyata.
entries.translation: O Allah, verily You have created my soul and You shall take it's life, to You belongs it's life and death. If You should keep my soul alive then protect it, and if You should take it's life then forgive it. O Allah, I ask You to grant me good health.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #103

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #104
entries.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ قِنِي عَذَابَكَ يَوْمَ تَبْعَثُ عِبَادَكَ)).
entries.transliteration: Allâhumma qinîcadhâbaka yawma tabcathucibâdaka.
entries.translation: O Allah, protect me from Your punishment on the day Your servants are resurrected. [three times]
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #104

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #105
entries.arabic: ((بِاسْمِكَ اللَّهُمَّ أَمُوتُ وَأَحْيَا)).
entries.transliteration: Bismika l-lâhumma amûtu wa ahyâ.
entries.translation: In Your name O Allah, I live and die.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #105

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #106
entries.arabic: ((سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ (ثلاثاً وثلاثين) وَالْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ (ثلاثاً وثلاثين) وَاللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ (أربعاً وثلاثينَ))).
entries.transliteration: Subhâna l-lâh , al-hamdu li-l-lâh , allâhu akbar .
entries.translation: How Perfect Allah is [thirty-three times]. All praise is for Allah [thirty-three times]. Allah is the greatest. [thirty-four times]
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #106

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #107
entries.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ رَبَّ السَّمَوَاتِ السَّبْعِ وَرَبَّ الأَرْضِ، وَرَبَّ الْعَرْشِ الْعَظِيمِ، رَبَّنَا وَرَبَّ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ، فَالِقَ الْحَبِّ وَالنَّوَى، وَمُنْزِلَ التَّوْرَاةِ وَالْإِنْجِيلِ، وَالْفُرْقَانِ، أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ أَنْتَ آخِذٌ بِنَاصِيَتِهِ. اللَّهُمَّ أَنْتَ الأَوَّلُ فَلَيْسَ قَبْلَكَ شَيْءٌ، وَأَنْتَ الآخِرُ فَلَيسَ بَعْدَكَ شَيْءٌ، وَأَنْتَ الظَّاهِرُ فَلَيْسَ فَوْقَكَ شَيْءٌ، وَأَنْتَ الْبَاطِنُ فَلَيْسَ دُونَكَ شَيْءٌ، اقْضِ عَنَّا الدَّيْنَ وَأَغْنِنَا مِنَ الْفَقْرِ)).
entries.transliteration: Allâhumma rabba s-samâwâti s-sabci wa rabba-l-carshi-l-cazîmi ! Rabbanâ wa rabba kulli shay'in ! Fâliqa-l-habbi wa n-nawâ wa munzila t-tawrâti wa-l-injîli wa-l-furqâni.Acûdhu bika min sharri kulli shay'in anta âkhidhun bi-nâsiyatihi. Allâhumma anta-l-awwalu fa-laysa qablaka shay'un. Wa anta-l-âkhiru fa-laysa bacdaka shay'un.Wa anta-z-zâhiru fa-laysa fawqaka shay'un. Wa anta-l-bâtinu fa-laysa dûnaka shay'un. Iqdicannâ d-dayna wa aghninâ mina-l-faqri.
entries.translation: O Allah, Lord of the seven heavens and the exalted throne, our Lord and Lord of all things, Splitter of the seed and the date stone, Revealer of the Tawrah, the Injeel and the Furqan, I take refuge in You from the evil of all things You shall seize by the forelock. O Allah, You are The First so there is nothing before You and You are The Last so there is nothing after You.You are Aththahir so there is nothing above You and You are Al-Batin so there is nothing closer than You.Settle our debt for us and spare us from poverty.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #107

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #108
entries.arabic: ((الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي أَطْعَمَنَا وَسَقَانَا، وَكَفَانَا، وَآوَانَا، فَكَمْ مِمَّنْ لاَ كَافِيَ لَهُ وَلاَ مُؤْوِيَ)).
entries.transliteration: Al hamdu li-l-lâhi l-lâdhi atcamanâ, wa saqânâ, wa kafânâ, wa âwânâ. Fa-kam mimman lâ kâfiya lahu wa lâ mu'wî.
entries.translation: All praise is for Allah, Who fed us and gave us drink, and Who is sufficient for us and has sheltered us, for how many have none to suffice them or shelter them.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #108

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #109
entries.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ عَالِمَ الغَيْبِ وَالشَّهَادَةِ فَاطِرَ السَّمَوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ، رَبَّ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ وَمَلِيكَهُ، أَشْهَدُ أَنْ لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ أَنْتَ، أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّ نَفْسِي، وَمِنْ شَرِّ الشَّيْطانِ وَشِرْكِهِ، وَأَنْ أَقْتَرِفَ عَلَى نَفْسِي سُوءاً، أَوْ أَجُرَّهُ إِلَى مُسْلِمٍ)).
entries.transliteration: Allâhummacâlim-alghaybi wa sh-shahâdati, fâtira s-samâwâti wa-l ardi,rabba kulli shay'in wa malîkahu. Ash-hadu anlâ ilâha illâ anta.Acûdhu bika min sharrin nafsî, wa min sharri sh-shaytâni wachirkihi,wa an aqtarifacalâ nafsî sû'an ajurrahu ilâ mouslim(in).
entries.translation: O Allah, Knower of the seen and the unseen, Creator of the heavens and the earth, Lord and Sovereign of all things I bear witness that none has the right to be worshipped except You. I take refuge in You from the evil of my soul and from the evil and shirk of the devil, and from committing wrong against my soul or bringing such upon another Muslim.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #109

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #110
entries.arabic: ((يَقْرَأُ ﴿الم﴾ تَنْزِيلَ السَّجْدَة ِ، وَتَبَارَكَ الَّذي بِيَدِهِ الْمُلْكُ)).
entries.transliteration: Yaqra'u Alif-Lam-Mim Tanzilas-Sajdah, wa Tabarakalladhi biyadihil-mulk.
entries.translation: Recite Surah As-Sajdah (Quran 32) and Surah Al-Mulk (Quran 67) before sleeping.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #110

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #111
entries.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ أَسْلَمْتُ نَفْسِي إِلَيْكَ، وَفَوَّضْتُ أَمْرِي إِلَيْكَ، وَوَجَّهْتُ وَجْهِي إِلَيْكَ، وَأَلْجَأْتُ ظَهْرِي إِلَيْكَ، رَغْبَةً وَرَهْبَةً إِلَيْكَ، لاَ مَلْجَأَ وَلاَ مَنْجَا مِنْكَ إِلاَّ إِلَيْكَ، آمَنْتُ بِكِتَابِكَ الَّذِي أَنْزَلْتَ، وَبِنَبِيِّكَ الَّذِي أَرْسَلْتَ)).
entries.transliteration: Allâhumma aslamtu nafsî ilayka, wa fawwadtu amrpi ilayka, wa wajjahtu wajhî ilayka, wa alja'tuzahrî ilayka.Raghbatan wa rahbatan ilayka. Âmantu bi-kitâbika l-ladhî anzalta wa bi-nabiyyika l-ladhî arsalta.
entries.translation: O Allah, I submit my soul unto You, and I entrust my affair unto You, and I turn my face towards You, and I totally rely on You, in hope and fear of You. Verily there is no refuge nor safe haven from You except with You. I believe in Your Book which You have revealed and in Your Prophet whom You have sent.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #111

categories.name: When tossing and turning during the night
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #112
entries.arabic: ((لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ الْوَاحِدُ الْقَهّارُ، رَبُّ السَّمَوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَمَا بَيْنَهُمَا الْعَزيزُ الْغَفَّارُ)).
entries.transliteration: Lâ ilâha illâ l-lâhu-l-wâhidu-l-qahhâru, rabbu s-samâwâti wa-l-ardi wa mâ baynahumâ-l-cazîzu-l-ghaffar.
entries.translation: There is none worthy of worship but Allah, the One, the Victorious, Lord of the heavens and the earth and all that is between them, the All-Mighty, the All-Forgiving.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #112 · This is to be said if you turn over in bed during the night. Al-Hakim graded it authentic and Ath-Thahabi agreed 1/540. Also see An-Nasa'i, 'Amalul-Yawm wal-Laylah, and Ibn As-Sunni. See also Al-Albani, Sahihul-Jami' As-Saghir 4/ 213.

categories.name: Upon experiencing unrest, fear, apprehensiveness during sleep
categories.arabicName: دعاء الفزع في النوم و من بُلِيَ بالوحشة
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #113
entries.arabic: ((أَعُوذُ بِكَلِمَاتِ اللَّهِ التَّامَّاتِ مِنْ غَضَبِهِ وَعِقَابِهِ، وَشَرِّ عِبَادِهِ، وَمِنْ هَمَزَاتِ الشَّياطِينِ وَأَنْ يَحْضُرُونِ)).
entries.transliteration: Acûdhu bi-kalimâti l-lâhi t-tâmmâti min ghadabihi, waciqâbihi, wa sharricibâdihi, wa min hamazâti sh-shayâtîni, wa an yahdurûni.
entries.translation: I seek refuge in the Perfect Words of Allah from His anger and His punishment, from the evil of His slaves, and from the taunts of devils and from their presence.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #113 · Abu Dawud 4/12. See also Al-Albani, Sahih At- Tirmidhi 3/171

categories.name: Upon seeing a good dream or a bad dream
categories.arabicName: ما يفعل من رأى الرؤيا أو الحلم
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 2 invocations.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #114
entries.arabic: [ (يَنْفُثُ عَنْ يَسَارِهِ (ثلاثاً)) - (يَسْتَعِيذُ بِاللَّهِ مِنَ الشَّيطَانِ وَمِنْ شَرِّ مَا رَأَى (ثَلاَثَ مَرَّاتٍ)) - ((لاَ يُحَدِّثْ بِهَا أَحَداً)) - ((يَــتَحَوَّلُ عَنْ جَنْبِهِ الَّذِي كَانَ عَلَيْهِ)) ]
entries.transliteration: Not applicable — this entry records Prophetic guidance rather than a separate recited formula.
entries.translation: Spit to your left (three times). 1 Seek refuge in Allah from the Devil and from the evil of what you have seen (three times). 2 Do not speak about it to anyone. 3 Turn over on your other side. 4
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #114 · 1. Muslim 4/1 772. 2. Muslim 4/1 772, 3. 3. Muslim 4/1772. 4. Muslim 4/1773.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #115
entries.arabic: ((ويَقُومُ يُصَلِّي إِنْ أَرَادَ ذَلِكَ)).
entries.transliteration: Not applicable — this entry records Prophetic guidance rather than a separate recited formula.
entries.translation: Get up and pray if you desire to do so.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #115 · Muslim 4/1773.

categories.name: Qunoot Al-Witr
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 3 invocations.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #116
entries.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ اهْدِنِي فِيمَنْ هَدَيْتَ، وَعَافِنِي فِيمَنْ عَافَيْتَ، وَتَوَلَّنِي فِيمَنْ تَوَلَّيْتَ، وَبَارِكْ لِي فِيمَا أَعْطَيْتَ، وَقِنِي شَرَّ مَا قَضَيْتَ؛ فَإِنَّكَ تَقْضِي وَلاَ يُقْضَى عَلَيْكَ، إِنَّهُ لاَ يَذِلُّ مَنْ وَالَيْتَ، [وَلاَ يَعِزُّ مَنْ عَادَيْتَ]، تَبارَكْتَ رَبَّنا وَتَعَالَيْتَ)).
entries.transliteration: Allâhumma hdinî fîman hadayta wacâfinî fîmancâfayta, wa tawallanî fîman tawallayta, wa bârik lî fîmâ actayta, wa qinî sharra mâ qadayta. Fa-innaka taqdî wa lâ yuqdâcalayka. Innahu lâ yadhillu man wâlayta [wa lâ yacizzu mancâdayta]. Tabârakata rabbanâ wa tacâlayta.
entries.translation: O Allah, guide me with those whom You have guided, and strengthen me with those whom You have given strength. Take me to Your care with those whom You have taken to Your care. Bless me in what You have given me. Protect me from the evil You have ordained. Surely, You command and are not commanded, and none whom You have committed to Your care shall be humiliated [and none whom You have taken as an enemy shall taste glory]. You are Blessed, Our Lord, and Exalted.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #116 · Abu Dawud, Ibn Majah, An-Nasa'i, At-Tirmidhi, Ahmad, Ad-Darimi, Al-Hakim, and Al-Bayhaqi. See also Al-Albani, Sahih At-Tirmidhi 1/144, Sahih Ibn Majah 1/194, and 'Irwa'ul-GhaW. 2/ 172.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #117
entries.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِرِضَاكَ مِنْ سَخَطِكَ، وَبِمُعَافَاتِكَ مِنْ عُقُوبَتِكَ، وَأَعُــــوذُ بِكَ مِنْكَ، لاَ أُحْصِي ثَنَاءً عَلَيْكَ، أَنْتَ كَمَا أَثْنَيْتَ عَلَى نَفْسِكَ)).
entries.transliteration: Allâhumma innî acudhu bi-ridâka min sakhattika, wa bi-mucâfâtika mincuqûbatika. Wa acûdhu bika minka. Lâ uhsthanâ'ancalayka, anta kama athnaytacalâ nafsik.
entries.translation: O Allah, I seek refuge with Your Pleasure from Your anger. I seek refuge in Your forgiveness from Your punishment. I seek refuge in You from You. I cannot count Your praises, You are as You have praised Yourself.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #117 · Abu Dawud, Ibn Majah, An-Nasa'i, At-Tirmidhi, Ahmad. See Al-Albani, Sahih At-Tirmidhi 3/180, Sahih Ibn Majah 1/194, and 'Irwa'ul-Ghalil. 2/ 175.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #118
entries.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ إِيَّاكَ نعْبُدُ، وَلَكَ نُصَلِّي وَنَسْجُدُ، وَإِلَيْكَ نَسْعَى وَنَحْفِدُ، نَرْجُو رَحْمَتَكَ، وَنَخْشَى عَذَابَكَ، إِنَّ عَذَابَكَ بِالكَافِرِينَ مُلْحَقٌ. اللَّهُمَّ إِنَّا نَسْتَعينُكَ، وَنَسْتَغْفِرُكَ، وَنُثْنِي عَلَيْكَ الْخَيْرَ، وَلاَ نَكْفُرُكَ، وَنُؤْمِنُ بِكَ، وَنَخْضَعُ لَكَ، وَنَخْلَعُ مَنْ يَكْفرُكَ)).
entries.transliteration: Allâhumma iyyâka nacbudu, wa laka nusallî wa nasjudu, wa ilayka nascâ wa nahfidu, najrû rahmataka wa nakhshâcadhâbaka, innacadhâbaka bi-l-kâfirîna mulhaqun. Allâhumma innâ nastacînuka wa nastaghfiruka, wa nuthnîcalayka-l-khayra wa lâ nakfuruka, wa nu'minu bika, wa nakhdacu laka, wa nakhlacu man yakfuruka.
entries.translation: O Allah, You alone do we worship, and to You we pray and bow down prostrate. To You we hasten to worship and to serve. Our hope is for Your mercy, and we fear Your punishment. Surely, Your punishment of the disbelievers is at hand. O Allah, we seek Your help and Your forgiveness, and we praise You beneficently. We do not deny You and we believe in You. We surrender to You and renounce whoever disbelieves in You.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #118 · Al-Bayhaqi graded its chain authentic in As-Sunan Al-Kubra. Al-Albani said in 'Irwa'ul-GhaliL 2/170 that its chain is authentic as a statement of 'Umar.

categories.name: Immediately after salam of the witr prayer
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #119
entries.arabic: ((سُبْحَانَ المَلِكِ القُدُّوسِ)) ثلاثَ مرَّاتٍ والثَّالِثَةُ يَجْهَرُ بها ويَمُدُّ بها صَوتَهُ يقولُ: [رَبِّ الْمَلاَئِكَةِ وَالرُّوحِ])).
entries.transliteration: Subhâna-l-maliki-l-quddûsi. [Rabbi-l-malâ'ikati wa r-rûhi.]
entries.translation: Glory is to the King, the Holy. [Recite three times in Arabic, and raise and extend the voice on the third time and say...] Lord of the angels and the Spirit.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #119 · An-Nasa'i 3/244, Ad-Daraqutni and others. The final addition is from Ad-Daraqutni's version 2/ 31 and its chain of narration is authentic. See the checking of Zadul-Ma'ad by Shu'aib Al-Arna'ut and 'Abdul-Qadir Al-Arna'ut 1/337.

categories.name: For anxiety and sorrow
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 2 invocations.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #120
entries.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي عَبْدُكَ، ابْنُ عَبْدِكَ، ابْنُ أَمَتِكَ، نَاصِيَتِي بِيَدِكَ، مَاضٍ فِيَّ حُكْمُكَ، عَدْلٌ فِيَّ قَضَاؤُكَ، أَسْأَلُكَ بِكُــــلِّ اسْمٍ هُوَ لَكَ، سَمَّيْتَ بِهِ نَفْسَكَ، أَوْ أَنْزَلْتَهُ فِي كِتَابِكَ، أَوْ عَلَّمْتَهُ أَحَداً مِنْ خَلْقِكَ، أَوِ اسْتَأْثَرْتَ بِهِ فِي عِلْمِ الغَيْبِ عِنْدَكَ، أَنْ تَجْعَلَ القُرْآنَ رَبِيعَ قَلْبِي، وَنُورَ صَدْرِي، وَجَلاَءَ حُزْنِي، وَذَهَابَ هَمِّي)).
entries.transliteration: Allâhumma innîcabduka, ibnucabdika, ibnu amatika. Nâsiyatî bi-yadika. Mâdin fiyyahukmuka,cadlun fiyya qadâ'uka. As'aluka bi-kulli smin huwa laka, sammayta bihi nafsaka, aw anzaltahu fî kitâbika, awcallamtahu ahadan min khalqika, aw ista'tharta bihi fîcilmi-l-ghaybicindaka, an tajcala-l-qur'âna rabîca qalbî, wa nûrassadrî, wa jalâ'ahuznî, wa dhahâba hammî.
entries.translation: O Allah, I am Your slave, and the son of Your male slave, and the son of your female slave. My forehead is in Your Hand (i.e. you have control over me). Your Judgment upon me is assured, and Your Decree concerning me is just. I ask You by every Name that You have named Yourself with, revealed in Your Book, taught any one of Your creation, or kept unto Yourself in the knowledge of the unseen that is with You, to make the Qur'an the spring of my heart, and the light of my chest, the banisher of my sadness, and the reliever of my distress.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #120 · Ahmad 1/391, and Al-Albani graded it authentic.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #121
entries.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنَ الْهَمِّ وَالْحَزَنِ، وَالْعَجْزِ وَالْكَسَلِ، وَالْبُخْلِ وَالْجُبْنِ، وَضَلَعِ الدَّيْنِ وَغَلَبَةِ الرِّجَالِ)).
entries.transliteration: Allâhumma innî acûdhu bika mina-l-hammi wa-l-hazani, wa-l-cajzi wa-l-kasali, wa-l-bukhli wa-l-jubni, wadalaci d-dayni wa ghalabati r-rijâl.
entries.translation: O Allah, I seek refuge in you from grief and sadness, from weakness and from laziness, from miserliness and from cowardice, from being overcome by debt and overpowered by men (i.e. others).
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #121 · Al-Bukhari 7/158. See also Al-Asqalani, Fathul-Bari 11/173.

categories.name: For one in distress
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 4 invocations.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #122
entries.arabic: ((لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ الْعَظِيمُ الْحَلِيمُ، لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ رَبُّ الْعَرْشِ الْعَظِيمِ، لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ رَبُّ السَّمَوَاتِ وَرَبُّ الْأَرْضِ وَرَبُّ الْعَرْشِ الْكَرِيمِ)).
entries.transliteration: Lâ ilâha illâ l-lâhu-l-cazîmu-l-halîmu. Lâ ilâha illâ l-lâhu, rabbu-l-carshi-l-cazîm. Lâ ilâha illâ l-lâhu, rabbu s-samâwâti wa rabbu-l-ardi wa rabbu-l-carshi-l-karîm.
entries.translation: There is none worthy of worship but Allah, the Mighty, the Forbearing. There is none worthy of worship but Allah, Lord of the Magnificent Throne. There is none worthy of worship but Allah, Lord of the heavens and Lord of the earth, and Lord of the Noble Throne.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #122 · Al-Bukhari 8/154, Muslim 4/2092

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #123
entries.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ رَحْمَتَكَ أَرْجُو، فَلاَ تَكِلْنِي إِلَى نَفْسِي طَرْفَةَ عَيْنٍ، وَأَصْلِحْ لِي شَأْنِي كُلَّهُ، لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ أَنْتَ)).
entries.transliteration: Allâhumma rahmataka arjû. Falâ takilnî ilâ nafsîtarfatacaynin, wa aslihlî sha'nî kullahu. Lâ ilâha illâ anta.
entries.translation: O Allah, I hope for Your mercy. Do not leave me to myself even for the blinking of an eye (i.e. a moment). Correct all of my affairs for me. There is none worthy of worship but You.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #123 · Abu Dawud 4/324, Ahmad 5/42. Al-Albani graded it as good in Sahih Abu Dawud 3/959.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #124
entries.arabic: ((لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ أَنْتَ سُبْحَانَكَ إِنِّي كُنْتُ مِنَ الظّالِمِينَ)).
entries.transliteration: Lâ ilâha illâ anta, subhânaka, innî kuntu minaz-zâlimîn.
entries.translation: There is none worthy of worship but You, glory is to You. Surely, I was among the wrongdoers.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #124 · At-Tirmidhi 5/529. Al-Hakim declared it authentic and Ath-Thahabi agreed with him 1/ 505. See also Al-Albani, Sahih At-Tirmidhi 3/168.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #125
entries.arabic: ((اللَّهُ اللَّهُ رَبِّي لاَ أُشْرِكُ بِهِ شَيْئاً)).
entries.transliteration: Allâhu, Allâhu rabbî. Lâ ushriku bihi shay'an.
entries.translation: Allah, Allah is my Lord. I do not associate anything with Him.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #125 · Abu Dawud 2/87. See also Al-Albani, Sahih Ibn Mdjah 2/335.

categories.name: Upon encountering an enemy or those of authority
categories.arabicName: دعاء لقاء العدو و ذي السلطان
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 3 invocations.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #126
entries.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ إِنَّا نَجْعَلُكَ فِي نُحُورِهِم، وَنَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شُرُورِهِمْ)).
entries.transliteration: Allâhumma innâ najcaluka fî nuhû rihim, wa nacûdhu bika min shurûrihim.
entries.translation: O Allah, we ask You to restrain them by their necks, and we seek refuge in You from their evil.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #126 · Abu Dawud 2/89, and Al-Hakim graded it authentic and Ath-Thahabi agreed 2/142.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #127
entries.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ أَنْتَ عَضُدِي، وَأَنْتَ نَصِيرِي، بِكَ أَحُولُ وَبِكَ أَصُولُ، وَبِكَ أُقاتِلُ)).
entries.transliteration: Allâhumma antacâdudî, wa anta nasîrî. Bika ajûlu, wa bika assûlu, wa bika uqâtil.
entries.translation: O Allah, You are my strength and You are my support. For Your sake, I go forth and for Your sake, I advance and for Your sake, I fight.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #127 · Abu Dawud 3/42, At-Tirmidhi 5/572. See also Al-Albani, Sahih At-Tirmidhi 3/183.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #128
entries.arabic: ((حَسْبُنا اللَّهُ وَنِعْمَ الْوَكِيلُ)).
entries.transliteration: Hasbuna l-lâhu, wa nicma-l-wakîl.
entries.translation: Allah is sufficient for us and the best of those on whom to depend.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #128 · Al-Bukhari, 5/172.

categories.name: For one afraid of the ruler's injustice
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 2 invocations.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #129
entries.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ ربَّ السَّمَوَاتِ السَّبْعِ، وَرَبَّ الْعَرْشِ الْعَظِيمِ، كُنْ لِي جَاراً مِنْ فُلاَنِ بْنِ فُلاَنٍ، وَأَحْزَابِهِ مِنْ خَلاَئِقِكَ، أَنْ يَفْرُطَ عَلَيَّ أَحَدٌ مِنْهُمْ أَوْ يَطْغَى، عَزَّ جَارُكَ، وَجَلَّ ثَنَاؤُكَ، وَلاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ أَنْتَ)).
entries.transliteration: Allâhumma rabba s-samâwâti s-sabci, wa rabba-l-carshi-l-cazîmi. Kun lî jâran min (citer son nom) wa ahzabihi min khalâ'iqika, an yafrutacalayya ahadun minhum wa yatghâ.cAzza jâruka, wa jalla thanâ'uka, wa lâ ilâha illâ ant.
entries.translation: O Allah, Lord of the seven heavens, Lord of the Magnificent Throne, be for me a support against [such and such a person] and his helpers from among your creatures, lest any of them abuse me or do me wrong. Mighty is Your patronage, and glorious are Your praises. There is none worthy of worship but You.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #129 · Al-Bukhari, Al-'Adab Al-Mufrad (no. 707). Al-Albani graded it authentic in Sahih Al-'Adab Al-Mufrad (no. 545).

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #130
entries.arabic: ((اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، اللَّهُ أَعَزُّ مِنْ خَلْقِهِ جَمِيعاً، اللَّهُ أَعَزُّ مِمَّا أَخَافُ وَأَحْذَرُ، أَعُوذُ بِاللَّهِ الَّذِي لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ هُوَ، الْمُمْسِكِ السَّمَوَاتِ السَّبْعِ أَنْ يَقَعْنَ عَلَى الْأَرْضِ إِلاَّ بِإِذْنِهِ، مِنْ شَرِّ عَبْدِكَ فُلاَنٍ، وَجُنُودِهِ وَأَتْبَاعِهِ وَأَشْيَاعِهِ، مِنْ الْجِنِّ وَالإِنْسِ، اللَّهُمَّ كُنْ لِي جَاراً مِنْ شَرِّهِمْ، جَلَّ ثَنَاؤُكَ وَعَزَّ جَارُكَ، وَتَبَارَكَ اسْمُكَ، وَلاَ إِلَهَ غَيْرُكَ)) (ثلاثَ مرَّاتٍ).
entries.transliteration: Allâhu akbaru. Allâhu acazzu min khalqihi jamîcan. Allâhu acazzu mimmâ akhâfu wa ahdharu. Acûdhu bi-l-lâhi l-ladhî lâ ilâha illâ huwa-l-mumsiki s-samâwâti s-sabci an yaqacnacalâ-l-ardi illâ bi-idhnihi, min sharricabdika (citer son nom) wa junûdihi wa atbâcihi wa ashyâcihi mina-l-jinni wa-l-insi. Allâhumma kun lî jâran min sharrihim. Jalla thanâ'uka wacazza âruka, wa tabâraka smuka, wa lâ ilâha ghayruka.
entries.translation: Allah is the Most Great, Mightier than all His creation. He is Mightier than what I fear and dread. I seek refuge in Allah, Who there is none worthy of worship but Him. He is the One Who holds the seven heavens from falling upon the earth except by His command. [I seek refuge in You Allah] from the evil of Your slave [name of the person], and his helpers, his followers, and his supporters from among the jinn and mankind. O Allah, be my support against their evil. Glorious are Your praises and mighty is Your patronage. Blessed is Your Name, there is no true God but You. (Recite three times in Arabic.)
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #130 · Al-Bukhari, 5/172.

categories.name: Against enemies
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #131
entries.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ مُنْزِلَ الْكِتَابِ، سَرِيعَ الْحِسَابِ، اهْزِمِ الأَحْزَابَ، اللَّهُمَّ اهزِمْهُمْ وَزَلْزِلْهُمْ)).
entries.transliteration: Allâhumma ! Munzila-l-kitâbi, sarîca-l-hisâbi ! Ihzimi-l-ahzâba. Allâhumma hzimhum wa zalzilhum.
entries.translation: O Allah, Revealer of the Book, Swift to account, defeat the groups (of disbelievers). O Allah, defeat them and shake them.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #131 · Muslim 3/1362.

categories.name: When being afraid of a group of people
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #132
entries.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ اكْفِنِيهِمْ بِمَا شِئْتَ)).
entries.transliteration: Allâhumma kfinîhim bimâ shi'ta.
entries.translation: O Allah, suffice (i.e. protect) me against them however You wish.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #132 · Muslim 4/2300.

categories.name: For one afflicted with doubt in his faith
categories.arabicName: دعاء من أصابه وسوسة في الإيمان
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 3 invocations.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #133
entries.arabic: ((يَسْتَعِيذُ بِاللَّهِ و يَنْتَهِي عَمَّا شك فِيهِ)).
entries.transliteration: Acûdhu bi-l-lâhi mina sh-shaytâni r-rajîm.
entries.translation: (Say:) I seek refuge in Allah. (Then you should desist from doing what you are in doubt about.)
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #133 · Al-Bukhari, cf. Al-Asqalani, Fathul-Bari 6/336, Muslim 1/120.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #134
entries.arabic: ((يَقُولُ: ((آمَنْتُ بِاللَّهِ وَرُسُلِهِ)).
entries.transliteration: Âmantu bi-l-lâhi wa rusûlihi.
entries.translation: (Say:) I believe in Allah and His Messengers.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #134 · Muslim, 1/119-20.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #135
entries.arabic: ((يَقْرَأُ قَوْلَهُ تَعَالَى: ﴿هُوَ الْأوَّلُ وَالْآخِرُ وَالظّاهِرُ وَالْباطِنُ وَهُوَ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ عَلِيمٌ﴾)).
entries.transliteration: Huwa-l-awwalu wa-l-âkhiru waz-zâhiru wa-l-bâtinu, wa huwa bi-kulli shay'incalîm.
entries.translation: (Recite the Ayat) He is the First and the Last, the Most High and the Most Near. And He is the Knower of all things (in Arabic).
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #135 · Al-Hadid 57:3, Abu Dawud 4/329. Al-Albani graded it good in SahihAbu Dawud, 3/962.

categories.name: Settling a debt
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 2 invocations.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #136
entries.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ اكْفِنِي بِحَلاَلِكَ عَنْ حَرَامِكَ، وَأَغْنِنِي بِفَضْلِكِ عَمَّنْ سِوَاكَ)).
entries.transliteration: Allâhumma kfinî bi-halâlikacanharâmika wa ghninî bi-fadlikacamman siwâk.
entries.translation: O Allah, suffice me with what You have allowed instead of what You have forbidden, and make me independent of all others besides You.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #136 · At-Tirmidhi 5/560. See also Al-Albani, Sahih At-Tirmidhi 3/180.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #137
entries.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنَ الْهَمِّ وَالْحَزَنِ، وَالْعَجْزِ وَالْكَسَلِ، وَالْبُخْلِ وَالْجُبْنِ، وَضَلَعِ الدَّيْنِ وَغَلَبَةِ الرِّجَالِ)).
entries.transliteration: Allâhumma innî acûdhu bika mina-l-hammi wa-l-hazani, wa-l-cajzi wa-l-kasali, wa-l-bukhli wa-l-jubni, waddalaci d-dayni wa ghalabati r-rijâl.
entries.translation: O Allah! I seek refuge with You from worry and grief, from incapacity and laziness, from cowardice and miserliness, from being heavily in debt and from being overpowered by (other) men.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #137 · Al-Bukhari 7/158.

categories.name: For one afflicted by whisperings in prayer or recitation
categories.arabicName: دعاء الوسوسة في الصلاة و القراءة
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #138
entries.arabic: ((أَعُوذُ بِاللَّهِ مِنَ الشَّيطَانِ الرَّجِيمِ، وَاتْفُلْ عَلَى يَسَارِكَ (ثلاثاً))).
entries.transliteration: Acûdhu bi-l-lâhi mina sh-shaytâni r-rajîm.
entries.translation: (Say:) I seek refuge in Allah from Satan the outcast (then blow with a little spittle to your left). (Do this three times reciting in Arabic.)
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #138 · Muslim 4/1729.

categories.name: For one whose affairs have become difficult
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #139
entries.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ لاَ سَهْلَ إِلاَّ مَا جَعَلْتَهُ سَهْلاً، وَأَنْتَ تَجْعَلُ الْحَزْنَ إِذَا شِئْتَ سَهْلاً)).
entries.transliteration: Allâhumma lâ sahla illâ mâ jacaltahu sahlan, wa anta tajcalu-l-hazana idhâ shi'ta sahlan.
entries.translation: O Allah, there is no ease other than what You make easy. If You please You ease sorrow.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #139 · Ibn Hibban in his Sahih (no. 2427), and Ibn As- Sunni (no. 351). Al-Hafidh (Ibn Hajar) said that this Hadith is authentic. It was also declared authentic by 'Abdul-Qadir Al-Arna'ut in his checking of An-Nawawi's Kitabul-Athkarp. 106.

categories.name: Upon committing a sin
categories.arabicName: ما يقول ويفعل من أذنب ذنبا
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #140
entries.arabic: ((مَا مِنْ عَبْدٍ يُذنِبُ ذَنْباً فَيُحْسِنُ الطُّهُورَ، ثُمَّ يَقُومُ فَيُصَلِّي رَكْعَتَيْنِ، ثُمَّ يَسْتَغْفِرُ اللَّهَ إِلاَّ غَفَرَ اللَّهُ لَهُ)).
entries.transliteration: Not applicable — this entry records Prophetic guidance rather than a separate recited formula.
entries.translation: There is not any slave of Allah who commits a sin, then he perfects his purification and stands to pray two Rak'ahs of prayer, then seeks Allah's forgiveness, except that Allah will forgive him.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #140 · Abu Dawud 2/86, At-Tirmidhi 2/257. Al-Albani graded it authentic in Sahih Abu Dawud 1/283.

categories.name: For expelling the devil and his whisperings
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 3 invocations.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #141
entries.arabic: ((الْاسْتِعَاذَةُ بِاللَّهِ مِنْهُ)).
entries.transliteration: Acûdhu bi-l-lâhi mina sh-shaytâni r-rajîm.
entries.translation: Seeking refuge with Allah against him (i.e. by saying I seek refuge in Allah from Satan the outcast).
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #141 · Abu Dawud 1/206, At-Tirmidhi. See also Al-Albani, Sahih At-Tirmidhi 1/77, and Surat Al-Mu'minun, 23:98-9.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #142
entries.arabic: ((الْأَذَانُ)).
entries.transliteration: Not applicable — this entry records Prophetic guidance rather than a separate recited formula.
entries.translation: The call to prayer - 'Athan.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #142 · Muslim 1/291, Al-Bukhari 1/151.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #143
entries.arabic: ((الْأَذْكَارُ وَقِرَاءَةُ الْقُرْآنِ)).
entries.transliteration: Not applicable — this entry records Prophetic guidance rather than a separate recited formula.
entries.translation: Saying words of Allah's remembrance (Thikr) and recitation of the Qur'an.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #143 · "Do not turn your homes into graveyards, surely the Devil flees from the house in which Surat Al-Baqarah is read," Muslim 1/539. The Devil is also driven out by the invocations for morning and evening, those that are said before sleeping and upon waking up, those for entering and leaving the house, including those for entering and leaving the mosque, and by many other authentic invocations taught to us by the Prophet (ﷺ) such as the reading of 'Ayatul-Kursi, (Al-Baqarah 2:255), and the last two 'Ayat of Surat Al-Baqarah before going to sleep. Whoever says: "There is none worthy of worship but Allah alone, Who has no partner, His is the dominion and His is the praise, and he is Able to do all things," one hundred times, it will be a protection for him from the Devil throughout the day.'

categories.name: When stricken with a mishap or overtaken by an affair
categories.arabicName: الدعاء حينما يقع ما لا يرضاه أو ُ غلب على أمره
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #144
entries.arabic: ((قَدَرُ اللَّهُ وَمَا شَاءَ فَعَلَ)).
entries.transliteration: Qaddaru l-lâhi wa mâ shâ'a facala.
entries.translation: It is the Decree of Allah and He does whatever He wills.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #144 · "The strong believer is better and more dear to Allah than the weak believer, and in each of them there is good. Be vigilant for what is to your benefit and seek the help of Allah and do not falter. But when you are striken by some setback, do not say: 'If only I had done such and such,' rather say: 'It is the Decree of Allah and He does whatever He wills.' For verily the saying 'if (i.e. if only I had) begins the work of the Devil." Muslim 4/2052.

categories.name: Congratulation on the occasion of a birth and its reply
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #145
entries.arabic: ((بَارَكَ اللَّهُ لَكَ فِي الْمَوْهُوبِ لَكَ، وَشَكَرْتَ الْوَاهِبَ، وَبَلَغَ أَشُدَّهُ، وَرُزِقْتَ بِرَّهُ)). وَيَرُدُّ عَلَيْهِ الْمُهَــــــنَّأُ فَيَقُولُ: ((بَارَكَ اللَّهُ لَكَ وَبَارَكَ عَلَيْكَ، وَجَزَاكَ اللَّهُ خَيْراً، وَرَزَقَكَ اللَّهُ مِثْلَهُ، وَأَجْزَلَ ثَوَابَكَ)).
entries.transliteration: Bâraka l-lâhu laka fî-l-mawhûbi laka, wa shakarta-l-wâhiba, wa balagha ashuddahu, wa ruziqta birrahu.Ce à quoi on répond :Bâraka l-lâhu laka, wa bârakacalayka, wa jazâka l-lâhu khayran, wa razaqaka l-lâhu mithlahu, wa ajzala thawâbaka.
entries.translation: May Allah bless you with His gift to you, and may you (the new parent) give thanks, may the child reach the maturity of years, and may you be granted its righteousness. The reply of the person being congratulated is to say: May Allah bless you, and shower His blessings upon you, and may Allah reward you well and bestow upon you its like and reward you abundantly
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #145 · An-Nawawi, Kitdbul-'Athkarp. 349, and Sahihul-'Athkar2/7l3 by Saleem Al-Hilali.

categories.name: Placing children under Allah's protection
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #146
entries.arabic: كَانَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صلى الله عليه وسلم يُعَوِّذُ الحَسَنَ وَالحُسَينَ رضي الله عنهما ((أُعِيذُكُمَا بِكَلِمَاتِ اللَّهِ التَّامَّةِ مِنْ كُلِّ شَيْطَانٍ وَهَامَّةٍ، وَمِنْ كُلِّ عَيْنٍ لاَمَّةٍ)).
entries.transliteration: Ucîdhukumâ bi-kalimâti l-lâhi t-tâmmati min kulli shaytânin wa hâmmatin, wa min kullicaynin lâmmatin.
entries.translation: I seek protection for you in the Perfect Words of Allah from every devil and every beast, and from every envious blameworthy eye.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #146 · Al-Bukhari4/119.

categories.name: When visiting the sick
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 2 invocations.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #147
entries.arabic: ((لاَ بأْسَ طَهُورٌ إِنْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ)).
entries.transliteration: Lâ ba'sa,tahûrun in shâ'a l-lâhu.
entries.translation: Do not worry, it will be a purification (for you), Allah willing.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #147 · Al-Bukhari, cf. Al-Asqalani Fathul-Bari 10/118.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #148
entries.arabic: ((أَسْأَلُ اللَّهَ الْعَظيمَ رَبَّ الْعَرْشِ الْعَظِيمِ أَنْ يَشْفيَكَ)) (سبع مرات).
entries.transliteration: As'alu l-lâha-l-cazîma, rabba-l-carshi-l-cazîmi, an yashfiyaka.
entries.translation: I ask Almighty Allah, Lord of the Magnificent Throne, to make you well. (Recite seven times in Arabic .)
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #148 · At-Tirmidhi, Abu Dawud. See also Al-Albani, Sahih At-Tirmidhi 2/210 and Sahihul-Jami' As-Saghir 5/180.

categories.name: Excellence of visiting the sick
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #149
entries.arabic: قَالَ النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((إِذَا عَادَ الرَّجُلُ أَخَاهُ الْمُسْلِمَ مَشَى فِي خِرَافَةِ الْجَنَّةِ حَتَّى يَجْلِسَ، فَإِذَا جَلَسَ غَمَرَتْهُ الرَّحْمَةُ، فَإِنْ كَانَ غُدْوَةً صَلَّى عَلَيْهِ سَبْعُونَ أَلْفَ مَلَكٍ حَتَّى يُمْسِيَ، وَإِنْ كَانَ مَسَاءً صَلَّى عَلَيْهِ سَبْعُونَ أَلْفَ مَلَكٍ حَتَّى يُصْبِحَ)).
entries.transliteration: Not applicable — this entry records Prophetic guidance rather than a separate recited formula.
entries.translation: When a man goes to visit his sick Muslim brother, he walks along a path of Paradise until he sits, and when he sits he is cloaked in mercy. If he comes in the morning, seventy thousand angels pray for him until evening, and if he comes in the evening, seventy thousand angels pray for him until morning.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #149

categories.name: When the sick have renounced all hope of life
categories.arabicName: دعاء المريض الذي يئس من حياته
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 3 invocations.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #150
entries.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ اغْفِرْ لِي، وَارْحَمْنِي، وَأَلْحِقْنِي بِالرَّفِيقِ الْأَعْلَى)).
entries.transliteration: Allâhumma ghfir lî, wa rhamnî, wa alhiqnî bi-r-rafîqi-l-aclâ.
entries.translation: O Allah, forgive me and have mercy upon me, and join me with the highest companions (in Paradise).
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #150 · Al-Bukhari7/10, Muslim 4/1893.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #151
entries.arabic: ((جَعَلَ النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم عِنْدَ مَوْتِهِ يُدْخِلُ يَدَيْهِ فِي الْمَاءِ فَيَمْسَحُ بِهِمَا وَجْهَهُ، وَيَقُولُ: لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ إِنَّ لِلْمَوْتِ سَكَرَاتٍ)).
entries.transliteration: Lâ ilâha illâ l-lâhu. Inna li-l-mawti la-sakarâtin.
entries.translation: There is none worthy of worship but Allah, surely death has agonies.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #151 · Al-Bukhari, cf. Al-Asqalani, Fathul-Bari 8/144. The Hadith also mention him using the Siivak (tooth stick).

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #152
entries.arabic: ((لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ وَاللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ، لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لاَ شَرِيكَ لَهُ، لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ لَهُ المُلْكُ وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ، لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ وَلاَ حَوْلَ وَلاَ قُوَّةَ إِلاَّ بِاللَّهِ)).
entries.transliteration: Lâ ilâha illâ l-lâhu wa l-lâhu akbar. Lâ ilâha illâ l-lâhu wahdahu. Lâ ilâha illâ l-lâhu wahdahu lâ sharîka lahu. Lâ ilâha illâ l-lâhu, lahu-l-mulku wa lahu-l-hamdu. Lâ ilâha illâ l-lâhu, wa lâhawla wa lâ quwwata illâ bi-l-lâhi.
entries.translation: There is none worthy of worship but Allah, Allah is the Most Great. None has the right to be worshipped but Allah alone. None has the right to be worshipped but Allah alone, Who has no partner. There is none worthy of worship but Allah, His is the dominion and His is the praise. There is none worthy of worship but Allah, there is no power and no might but by Allah.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #152 · At-Tirmidhi and Ibn Majah. See also Al-Albani, Sahih At-Tirmidhi 3/152 and Sahih Ibn Majah 2/317.

categories.name: Instruction for the one nearing death
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #153
entries.arabic: ((مَنْ كَانَ آخِرُ كَلاَمِهِ لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ دَخَلَ الْجَنَّةَ)).
entries.transliteration: Lâ ilâha illâ l-lâhu
entries.translation: Whoever dies with the last words (whose meaning is): "There is none worthy of worship but Allah" will enter Paradise.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #153 · Abu Dawud 3/190. See also Al-Albani, Sahihul-Jami' As-Saghir 5/432.

categories.name: For one afflicted by a calamity
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #154
entries.arabic: ((إِنَّا لِلَّهِ وَإِنَّا إِلَيْهِ رَاجِعُونَ، اللَّهُمَّ أْجُرْنِي فِي مُصِيبَتِي، وَأَخْلِفْ لِي خَيْرَاً مِنْهَا)).
entries.transliteration: Innâ li-l-lâhi wa innâ ilayhi râjicuna. Allahumma 'jurnî musîbatî wa khluf lî khayran minhâ.
entries.translation: We are from Allah and unto Him we return. O Allah take me out of my plight and bring to me after it something better.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #154 · Muslim 2/632.

categories.name: When closing the eyes of the deceased
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #155
entries.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ اغْفِرْ لِفُلاَنٍ (بِاسْمِهِ) وَارْفَعْ دَرَجَتَهُ فِي الْمَهْدِيِّينَ، وَاخْلُفْهُ فِي عَقِبِهِ فِي الْغَابِرِينَ، وَاغْفِرْ لَنَا وَلَهُ يَا رَبَّ الْعَالَمِينَ، وَافْسَحْ لَهُ فِي قَبْرِهِ، وَنَوِّرْ لَهُ فِيهِ)).
entries.transliteration: Allâhumma ghfir li-fulânin (citer son nom) wa rfacdarajatahu fî-l-mahdiyyîna, wa khlufhu fîcaqibihi fî-l-ghâbirîna. Wa ghfir lanâ wa lahu, yâ rabba-l-câlamîna, wa fsahlahu fî qabrihi wa nawwir lahu fîhi.
entries.translation: O Allah, forgive [name of the person] and elevate his station among those who are guided. Send him along the path of those who came before, and forgive us and him, O Lord of the worlds. Enlarge for him his grave, and shed light upon him in it.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #155 · Muslim 2/634.

categories.name: For the deceased at the funeral prayer
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 4 invocations.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #156
entries.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ اغْفِرْ لَهُ وَارْحَمْهُ، وَعَافِهِ، وَاعْفُ عَنْهُ، وَأَكْرِمْ نُزُلَهُ، وَوَسِّعْ مُدْخَلَهُ، وَاغْسِلْهُ بِالْمَاءِ وَالثَّلْجِ وَالْبَرَدِ، وَنَقِّهِ مِنَ الْخَطَايَا كَمَا نَقَّيْتَ الثَّوْبَ الأَبْيَضَ مِنَ الدَّنَسِ، وَأَبْدِلْهُ دَاراً خَيْراً مِنْ دَارِهِ، وَأَهْلاً خَيْراً مِنْ أَهْلِهِ، وَزَوْجَاً خَيْراً مِنْ زَوْجِهِ، وَأَدْخِلْهُ الْجَنَّةَ، وَأَعِذْهُ مِنْ عَذَابِ القَبْرِ [وَعَذَابِ النَّارِ])).
entries.transliteration: Allâhumma ghfir lahu wa rhamhu wacâfihi, wacfucanhu, wa akrim nuzulahu, wa wassicmudkhalahu, wa ghsilhu bi-l-mâ'i wa th-thalji wa-l-baradi. Wa naqqihi mina-l-khatâya kamâ naqqayta th-thawba-l-abyada mina d-danasi. Wa abdilhu dâran khayran min dârihi, wa ahlan khayran min ahlihi, wa zawjan khayran min zawjihi. Wa dkhilhu-l-jannata wa acidh-hu mincadhâbi-l-qaqbr [wacadhâbi n-nâr].
entries.translation: O Allah, forgive him and have mercy on him, and give him strength and pardon him. Be generous to him, and cause his entrance to be wide, and wash him with water and snow and hail. Cleanse him of his transgressions as white cloth is cleansed of stains. Give him an abode better than his home, and a family better than his family, and a wife better than his wife. Take him into Paradise, and protect him from the punishment of the grave [and from the punishment of Hell-fire].
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #156 · Muslim 2/663.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #157
entries.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ اغْفِرْ لِحَيِّنَا وَمَيِّتِنَا، وَشَاهِدِنَا وَغَائِبِنَا، وَصَغِيرِنَا وَكَبيرِنَا، وَذَكَرِنَا وَأُنْثَانَا. اللَّهُمَّ مَنْ أَحْيَيْتَهُ مِنَّا فَأَحْيِهِ عَلَى الْإِسْلاَمِ، وَمَنْ تَوَفَّيْتَهُ مِنَّا فَتَوَفَّهُ عَلَى الإِيمَانِ، اللَّهُمَّ لاَ تَحْرِمْنَا أَجْرَهُ، وَلاَ تُضِلَّنَا بَعْدَهُ)).
entries.transliteration: Allâhumma ghfir li-hayyinâ wa mayyitinâ, wa shâhidinâ wa ghâ'ibinâ, wasaghîrinâ wa kabîrinâ, wa dhakarinâ wa unthânâ. Allâhumma man ahyaytahu minnâ fa-ahyihicalâ-l-islâmi. Wa man tawaffaytahu minnâ fa-tawaffahucalâ-l-îmâni. Allâhumma lâ tahrimnâ ajrahu wa la tudillabâ bacdahu.
entries.translation: O Allah forgive our living and our dead, those who are with us and those who are absent, our young and our old, our menfolk and our womenfolk. O Allah, whomever you give life from among us give him life in Islam, and whomever you take away from us take him away in Faith. O Allah, do not forbid us their reward and do not send us astray after them.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #157 · Ibn Majah 1/480, Ahmad 2/368. See also Al-Albani, Sahih Ibn Majah 1/251.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #158
entries.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ إِنَّ فُلاَنَ بْنَ فُلاَنٍ فِي ذِمَّتِكَ، وَحَبْلِ جِوَارِكَ، فَقِهِ مِنْ فِتْنَةِ الْقَبْرِ، وَعَذَابِ النَّارِ، وَأَنْتَ أَهْلُ الْوَفَاءِ وَالْحَقِّ، فَاغْفِرْ لَهُ وَارْحَمْهُ إِنَّكَ أَنْتَ الغَفُورُ الرَّحيمُ)).
entries.transliteration: Allâhumma inna (citer le nom) fî dhimmatika wahabli jiwârika. Fa-qihi min fitnati-l-qabri wacadhâbi n-nâri. Wa anta ahlu-l-wafâ'i wa-l-hhaqqi. Fa-ghfir lahu wa rhamhu, innaka anta-l-ghafûru r-rahîm.
entries.translation: O Allah, surely [name the person] is under Your protection, and in the rope of Your security, so save him from the trial of the grave and from the punishment of the Fire. You fulfill promises and grant rights, so forgive him and have mercy on him. Surely You are Most Forgiving, Most Merciful.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #158 · Ibn Majah, Abu Dawud 3/211. See also Al Albani, Sahih Ibn Majah 1/251.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #159
entries.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ عَبْدُكَ وَابْنُ أَمَتِكَ احْتَاجَ إِلَى رَحْمَتِكَ، وَأَنْتَ غَنِيٌّ عَنْ عَذَابِهِ، إِنْ كَانَ مُحْسِناً فَزِدْ فِي حَسَنَاتِهِ، وَإِنْ كَانَ مُسِيئاً فَتَجَاوَزْ عَنْهُ)).
entries.transliteration: Allâhummacabduka wa bnu amatika ihtâja ilâ rahmatika. Wa anta ghaniyyuncancadhâbihi. In kâna muhsinan, fa-zid fîhasanâtihi. Wa in kâna musî'an fa-tajâwazcanhu.
entries.translation: O Allah, Your male slave and the child of Your female slave is in need of Your mercy, and You are not in need of his torment. If he was pious then increase his rewards and if he was a transgressor then pardon him.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #159 · Al-Hakim 1/359 who graded it authentic and Ath-Thahabi agreed with him. See also Al-Albani, Ahkamul-Jana'iz, p. 125.

categories.name: When the deceased is a child, during the funeral prayer
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 2 invocations.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #160
entries.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ أَعِذْهُ مِنْ عَذَابِ القَبْرِ)) وإن قال: ((اللَّهُمَّ اجْعَلْهُ فَرَطاً وَذُخْراً لِوَالِدَيْهِ، وَشَفِيعاً مُجَاباً، اللَّهُمَّ ثَقِّلْ بِهِ مَوَازِينَهُمَا، وَأَعْظِمْ بِهِ أُجورَهُمَا، وَأَلْحِقْهُ بِصَالِحِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ، وَاجْعَلْهُ فِي كَفَالَةِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، وَقِهِ بِرَحْمَتِكَ عَذَابَ الْجَحِيمِ، وَأَبْدِلْهُ دَاراً خَيْراً مِنْ دَارِهِ، وَأَهْلاً خَيْراً مِنْ أَهْلِهِ، اللَّهُمَّ اغْفِرْ لِأَسْلاَفِنَا، وَأَفْرَاطِنَا، وَمَنْ سَبَقَنَا بِالْإِيمَانِ)) إن قال ذلك فَحَسَنٌ.
entries.transliteration: Allâhumma acidh-hu mincadhâbi-l-qabr.Allâhumma jcalhu faratan wa dhukran li-wâlidayhi, wa shafîcan mujâban. Allâhumma thaqqil bihi mawâzînahumâ, wa aczim bihi ijûrahumâ, wa alhiqhu bi-sâlihi-l-mu'minîna. Wa jcalhu fî kafâlati Ibrâhîma, wa qihi bi-rahmatikacadhâba-l-jahîmi. Wa abdilhu dâran khayran min dârihi, wa ahlan khayran min ahlihi. Allâhumma ghfir li-aslâfinâ wa afrâtinâ wa man sabaqanâ bi-l-îmân
entries.translation: O Allah, protect him from the torment of the grave. [It is also good to say:] O Allah, make him a precursor, a forerunner and a treasure for his parents and an answered intercessor. O Allah, make him weigh heavily in their scales (of good) and magnify their reward. Make him join the righteous of the believers. Place him in the care of Ibrahim. Save him by Your mercy from the torment of Hell. Give him a home better than his home, and a family better than his family. O Allah, forgive those who have gone (i.e. passed away) before us, our children lost (by death), and those who have preceded us in Faith.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #160 · Ibn Qudamah, Al-Mughni 3/416 and Ad-Duroosul-Muhimmah li-Aammatil-'Ummah, pg. 15, by Shaikh 'Abdul-'Aziz bin Baz.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #161
entries.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ اجْعَلْهُ لَنَا فَرَطاً، وَسَلَفاً، وَأَجْراً)).
entries.transliteration: Allâhumma jcalhu lanâ faratan wa salafan wa ajran.
entries.translation: O Allah, make him for us a precursor, a forerunner and a cause of reward.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #161 · Al-Hasan (Al-Basri) used to recite Surat Al-Fatihah for a child's funeral and then say this. Al-Bukhari, Kitabul-Jana'iz, p. 65.

categories.name: Condolence
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #162
entries.arabic: ((إِنَّ للَّهِ مَا أَخَذَ، وَلَهُ مَا أَعْطَى، وَكُلُّ شَيْءٍ عِنْدَهُ بِأَجَلٍ مُسَمَّى... فَلْتَصْبِرْ وَلْتَحْتَسِبْ)). وَإِنْ قَالَ: ((أَعْظَمَ اللَّهُ أَجْرَكَ، وَأَحْسَنَ عَزَاءَكَ، وَغَفَرَ لِمَيِّتِكَ)) فَحَسَنٌ.
entries.transliteration: Inna li-l-lâhi mâ akhadha wa li-l-lâhi mâ actâ, wa kullu shay'incindahu bi-ajalin musammân... Fa-l-tasbir wa-l-tahtasib.Aczama l-lâhu ajraka, wa ahsanacazâ'ka, wa ghafara li-mayyitika.
entries.translation: Surely, Allah takes what is His, and what He gives is His, and to all things He has appointed a time... so have patience and be rewarded.1 May Allah magnify your reward, and make perfect your bereavement, and forgive your departed.2
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #162 · 1 Al-Bukhari 2/80, Muslim 2/636. 2An-Nawawi, Kitabul-'Athkar, p. 126.

categories.name: Placing the deceased in the grave
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #163
entries.arabic: ((بِسْمِ اللَّهِ وَعَلَى سُنَّةِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ)).
entries.transliteration: Bismi l-lâhi wacalâ sunnati rasûli l-lâhi.
entries.translation: With the Name of Allah, and according to the Sunnah of the Messenger of Allah.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #163 · Abu Dawud 3/314 with an authentic chain. Ahmad also recorded it with the wording: With the Name of Allah, and according to the religion of the Messenger of Allah. Its chain is also authentic.

categories.name: After burying the deceased
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #164
entries.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ اغْفِرْ لَهُ، اللَّهُمَّ ثَبِّتْهُ)).
entries.transliteration: Allâhumma ghfir lahu, Allâhumma thabbit-hu.
entries.translation: O Allah, forgive him. O Allah, strengthen him.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #164 · The Prophet (ﷺ) used to stop after burying the dead and say to the people: "Ask Allah to forgive your brother and pray for him to be strengthened, for indeed he is now being questioned." Abu Dawud 3/315, and Al-Hakim 1/370 who graded it authentic and Ath-Thahabi agreed.

categories.name: Visiting the graves
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #165
entries.arabic: ((السَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكُمْ أَهْلَ الدِّيَارِ، مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَالْمُسْلِمِينَ، وَإِنَّا إِنْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ بِكُمْ لاَحِقُونَ، [وَيَرْحَمُ اللَّهُ الْمُسْتَقدِمِينَ مِنَّا وَالْمُسْتأْخِرِينَ] أَسْاَلُ اللَّهَ لَنَا وَلَكُمُ الْعَافِيَةَ)).
entries.transliteration: As-salâmucalaykum ahla d-diyâri mina-l-muminîna wa-l-muslimîna. Wa innâ in shâ'a l-lâhu bikum lâhiqûna [wa yarhamu l-lâhu-l-mustaqdimîna minnâ wa-l-musta'khirîna.] As'alu l-lâha lanâ wa lakumu-l-câfiyata.
entries.translation: Peace be upon you, people of this abode, from among the believers and those who are Muslims, and we, by the Will of Allah, shall be joining you. [May Allah have mercy on the first of us and the last of us] I ask Allah to grant us and you well-being.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #165 · Muslim 2/671, Ibn Majah 1/494, the portion brackets is from Muslim 2/671.

categories.name: During a wind storm
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 2 invocations.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #166
entries.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْــــــأَلُكَ خَيْرَهَا، وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّهَا)).
entries.transliteration: Allâhumma innî as'aluka khayrahâ wa acûdhu bika min sharrihâ.
entries.translation: O Allah, I ask You for the good of it, and seek refuge in You against its evil.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #166 · Abu Dawud 4/326, Ibn Majah 2/1228. See also Al-Albani, Sahih Ibn Majah 2/305.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #167
entries.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ خَيْرَهَا، وَخَيْرَ مَا فِيهَا، وَخَيْرَ مَا أُرْسِلَتْ بِهِ، وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّهَا، وَشَرِّ مَا فِيهَا، وَشَرِّ مَا أُرْسِلَتْ بِهِ)).
entries.transliteration: Allâhumma innî as'aluka khayrahâ wa khayra mâ fîhâ, wa khayra mâ ursilat bihi. Wa acûdhu bika min sharrihâ, wa sharri mâ fîhâ, wa sharri mâ ursilat bihi.
entries.translation: O Allah, I ask You for the good of it, for the good of what it contains, and for the good of what is sent with it. I seek refuge in You from the evil of it, from the evil of what it contains, and from the evil that is sent with it.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #167 · Muslim 2/616, Al-Bukhari 4/76.

categories.name: Upon hearing thunder
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #168
entries.arabic: ((سُبْحَانَ الَّذِي يُسَبِّحُ الرَّعْدُ بِحَمْدِهِ وَالْمَلاَئِكةُ مِنْ خِيفَتِهِ)).
entries.transliteration: Subhâna l-ladhî yusabbihu r-racdu bi-hamdihi wa-l-malâ'ikatu min khîfatihi.
entries.translation: Glory is to Him Whom thunder and angels glorify due to fear of Him.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #168 · Whenever Abdullah bin Zubair (RA) would hear thunder, he would abandon all conversation and say this supplication. See Al-Muwatta' 2/992. It was graded authentic by Al-Albani as a statement of Abdullah bin Zubayr only.

categories.name: For rain
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 3 invocations.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #169
entries.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ اسْقِنَا غَيْثاً مُغِيثاً مَرِيئاً مَرِيعاً، نَافِعاً غَيْرَ ضَارٍّ، عَاجِلاً غَيْرَ آجِلٍ)).
entries.transliteration: Allâhumma sqinâ ghaytan mughîthan, marî'an, murîcan, nâfican ghayradârrin,câjilan ghayra âjilin.
entries.translation: O Allah, shower upon us abundant rain, beneficial not harmful, swiftly and not delayed.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #169 · Abu Dawud 1/303. See also Al-Albani SahihAbu Dawud 1/216.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #170
entries.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ أَغِثْنَا، اللَّهُمَّ أَغِثْنَا، اللَّهُمَّ أَغِثْنَا)).
entries.transliteration: Allâhumma aghithnâ ! Allâhumma aghithnâ ! Allâhumma aghithnâ !
entries.translation: O Allah, send us rain. O Allah, send us rain. O Allah, send us rain.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #170 · Al-Bukhari 1/224, Muslim 2/613.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #171
entries.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ اسْقِ عِبَادَكَ، وَبَهَائِمَكَ، وَانْشُرْ رَحْمَتَكَ، وَأَحْيِي بَلَدَكَ الْمَيِّتَ)).
entries.transliteration: Allâhumma sqicibâdaka wa bahâ'imaka. Wa bshur rahmataka, wa ahyi baladaka-l-mayyita.
entries.translation: O Allah, give water to Your slaves, and Your livestock, and spread Your mercy, and revive Your dead land.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #171 · Abu Dawud 1/305. Al-Albani graded it good in Sahih Abu Dawud 1/218.

categories.name: When it rains
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #172
entries.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ صَيِّباً نَافِعاً)).
entries.transliteration: Allâhummasayyiban nâfican.
entries.translation: O Allah, (bring) beneficial rain clouds.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #172 · Al-Bukhari, cf. Al-Asqalani, Fathul-Bari 2/518.

categories.name: After rainfall
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #173
entries.arabic: ((مُطِرْنَا بِفَضْلِ اللَّهِ وَرَحْمَتِهِ)).
entries.transliteration: Mutirnâ bi-fadli l-lâhi wa rahmatihi.
entries.translation: We have been given rain by the grace and mercy of Allah.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #173 · Al-Bukhari 1/205, Muslim 1/83.

categories.name: Asking for clear skies
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #174
entries.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ حَوَالَيْنَا وَلاَ عَلَيْنَا، اللَّهُمَّ عَلَى الآكَامِ وَالظِّرَابِ، وَبُطُونِ الْأَوْدِيَةِ، وَمَنَابِتِ الشَّجَرِ)).
entries.transliteration: Allâhummahawâlaynâ wa lâcalaynâ. Allâhummacalâ-l-âkâmi waz-zirâbi wa butûni-l-awdiyati wa manâbiti sh-shajari.
entries.translation: O Allah, let the rain fall around us and not upon us, O Allah, (let it fall) on the pastures, hills, valleys, and the roots of trees.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #174 · Al-Bukhari 1/224, Muslim 1/614

categories.name: Upon sighting the crescent moon
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #175
entries.arabic: ((اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، اللَّهُمَّ أَهِلَّهُ عَلَيْنَا بِالْأَمْنِ وَالْإِيمَانِ، وَالسَّلاَمَةِ وَالْإِسْلاَمِ، وَالتَّوْفِيقِ لِمَا تُحِبُّ رَبَّنَا وَتَرْضَى، رَبُّنَا وَرَبُّكَ اللَّهُ)).
entries.transliteration: Allâhu akbaru. Allâhumma ahillahucalaynâ bi-l-amni, wa-l-îmâni, wa s-salâmati, wa-l-islâmi, wa t-tawfîqi limâ tuhibbu rabbanâ wa tardâ. Rabbunâ wa rabbuka l-lâhu.
entries.translation: Allah is the Most Great. O Allah, bring us the new moon with security and Faith, with peace and in Islam, and in harmony with what our Lord loves and what pleases Him. Our Lord and your Lord is Allah.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #175 · At-Tirmidhi 5/504, Ad-Darimi 1/336. See also Al-Albani, Sahih At-Tirmidhi 3/157.

categories.name: Upon breaking fast
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 2 invocations.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #176
entries.arabic: ((ذَهَبَ الظَّمَأُ وَابْتَلَّتِ العُرُوقُ، وَثَبَتَ الْأَجْرُ إِنْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ)).
entries.transliteration: Dhahabaz-zama'u wa btallati-l-curûqu wa thabata-l-ajru in shâ'a l-lâhu.
entries.translation: The thirst is gone, the veins are moistened, and the reward is confirmed, if Allah wills.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #176 · Abu Dawud 2/306 and others. See also Al- Albani, Sahihul-Jami' As-Saghir 4/209.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #177
entries.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ بِرَحْمَتِكَ الَّتِي وَسِعَتْ كُلَّ شَيْءٍ أَنْ تَغْفِرَ لِي)).
entries.transliteration: Allâhumma innî as'aluka bi-rahmatika l-lâti wasicat kulla shay'in an taghfira lî.
entries.translation: O Allah, I ask You by Your mercy, which encompasses all things, that You forgive me.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #177 · Ibn Majah 1/557 from a supplication of Abdullah bin 'Amr. Al-Hafidh graded it as good in his checking of An-Nawawi's Kitabul-'Athkdr. See Sharhul- Athkar 4/342.

categories.name: Before eating
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 2 invocations.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #178
entries.arabic: ((إِذَا أَكَلَ أَحَدُكُمْ طَعَاماً فَلْيَقُلْ بِسْمِ اللَّهِ، فَإِنْ نَسِيَ فِي أَوَّلِهِ فَلْيَقُلْ بسمِ اللَّهِ فِي أَوَّلِهِ وَآخِرِهِ)).
entries.transliteration: Au début du repas : Bismi l-lâhi.En cas d'oubli : Bismi l-lâhi fî awwalihi wa âkhirihi.
entries.translation: When anyone of you begins eating, say: With the Name of Allah. And if you forget then, when you remember, say: With the Name of Allah, in the beginning, and in the end.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #178 · Abu Dawud 3/347, At-Tirmidhi 4/288. See Al- Albani's Sahih At-Tirmidhi 2/167.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #179
entries.arabic: ((مَنْ أَطْعَمَهُ اللَّهُ الطَّعَامَ فَلْيَقُلْ: اللَّهُمَّ بَارِكْ لَنَا فِيهِ وَأَطْعِمْنَا خَيْراً مِنْهُ، وَمَنْ سَقَاهُ اللَّهُ لَبَناً فَلْيَقُلْ اللَّهُمَّ بَارِكْ لَنَا فِيهِ وَزِدْنَا مِنْهُ)).
entries.transliteration: Allâhumma bârik lanâ fîhi, wa atcimnâ khayran minhu.Allâhumma bârik lanâ fîhi wa zidnâ minhu.
entries.translation: Whomever Allah has given food, should say: O Allah, bless us in it and provide us with better than it. Whomever Allah has given milk to drink, should say : O Allah, bless us in it and give us more of it.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #179 · At-Tirmidhi 5/506. See also Al-Albani, Sahih At-Tirmidhi 3/158.

categories.name: Upon completing the meal
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 2 invocations.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #180
entries.arabic: ((الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي أَطْعَمَنِي هَذَا، وَرَزَقَنِيهِ، مِنْ غَيْرِ حَوْلٍ مِنِّي وَلاَ قُوَّةٍ)).
entries.transliteration: Alhamdu li-l-lâhi ladhî atcamanî hâdhâ wa razaqanîhi min ghayrinhawlin minnî wa lâ quwwatin.
entries.translation: Praise is to Allah Who has given me this food, and sustained me with it though I was unable to do it and powerless.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #180 · At-Tirmidhi, Abu Dawud, and Ibn Majah. See also Al-Albani, Sahih At-Tirmidhi 3/159.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #181
entries.arabic: ((الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ حَمْداً كَثِيراً طَيِّباً مُبَارَكاً فِيهِ، غَيْرَ [مَكْفِيٍّ وَلاَ ] مُوَدَّعٍ، وَلاَ مُسْتَغْنَىً عَنْهُ رَبَّنَا)).
entries.transliteration: Alhamdu li-l-lâhihamdan kathîran,tayyiban, mubârakan fîhi, ghayra makfiyyin wa lâ muwaddacin, wa lâ mustaghnancanhu rabbanâ.
entries.translation: All praise is to Allah, praise in abundance, good and blessed. It cannot [be compensated for, nor can it] be left, nor can it be done without, our Lord.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #181 · Al-Bukhari 6/214, At-Tirmidhi 5/507.

categories.name: Of the guest for the host
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #182
entries.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ بَارِكْ لَهُمْ فِيمَا رَزَقْتَهُم، وَاغْفِرْ لَهُمْ وَارْحَمْهُمْ)).
entries.transliteration: Allâhumma bârik lahum fîmâ razaqtahum wa ghfir lahum wa rhamhum.
entries.translation: O Allah, bless them in what You have provided for them, and forgive them and have mercy on them.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #182 · Muslim 3/1615.

categories.name: To one who intends to give food or drink
categories.arabicName: التعريض بالدعاء لطلب الطعام أو الشراب
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #183
entries.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ أَطْعِمْ مَنْ أَطْعَمَنِي، وَاسْقِ مَنْ سَقَانِي)).
entries.transliteration: Allâhumma atcim man atcamanî wa sqi man saqânî.
entries.translation: O Allah, feed the one who has fed me, and give drink to the one who has given me drink.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #183 · Muslim 3/126.

categories.name: When breaking fast in someone's home
categories.arabicName: الدعاء إذا أفطر عند أهل بيت
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #184
entries.arabic: ((أَفْطَرَ عِنْدَكُمُ الصَّائِمُونَ، وَأَكَلَ طَعَامَكُمُ الْأَبْرَارُ، وَصَلَّتْ عَلَيْكُمُ الْمَلاَئِكَةُ)).
entries.transliteration: Aftaracindakumus-sâ'imûna, wa akalatacâmakumu-l-abrâru, wasallatcalaykumu-l-malâ'ikatu.
entries.translation: With you, those who are fasting have broken their fast, you have fed those who are righteous, and the angels recite their prayers upon you.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #184 · Abu Dawud 3/367, Ibn Majah 1/556, An-Nasa'i, 'Amalul-Yawm wal-Laylah 296-8. Al-Albani graded it authentic in Sahih Abu Dawud 2/730.

categories.name: By one fasting when presented with food and does not break his fast
categories.arabicName: دعاء الصائم إذا حضر الطعام ولم يفطر
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #185
entries.arabic: ((إِذَا دُعِيَ أَحَدُكُمْ فَلْيُجِبْ، فَإِنْ كَانَ صَائِماً فَلْيُصَلِّ، وَإِنْ كَانَ مُفْطِراً فَلْيَطْعَمْ)) ، وَمَعْنَى فَلْيُصَلِّ أَيْ فَلْيَدْعُ.
entries.transliteration: Not applicable — this entry records Prophetic guidance rather than a separate recited formula.
entries.translation: When you are invited (to eat) then reply to the invitation. If you are fasting then invoke Allah's blessings (on your host), and if you are not fasting then eat.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #185 · Muslim 2/1054.

categories.name: When insulted while fasting
categories.arabicName: ما يقول الصائم إذا سابه أحد
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #186
entries.arabic: ((إِنِّي صَائِمٌ، إِنِّي صَائِمٌ)).
entries.transliteration: Innîsâimun ! Innîsâimun !
entries.translation: I am fasting. I am fasting.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #186 · Al-Bukhari, cf. Al-Asqalani, Fathul-BAri 4/103, Muslim 2/806.

categories.name: Upon seeing the early or premature fruit
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #187
entries.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ بَارِكْ لَنَا فِي ثَمَرِنَا، وَبَارِكْ لَنَا فِي مَدِينَتِنَا، وَبَارِكْ لَنَا فِي صَاعِنَا، وَبَارِكْ لَنَا فِي مُدِّنَا)).
entries.transliteration: Ammâhumma bârik lanâ fî thamarinâ, wa bârik lanâ fî madînatinâ, wa bârik lanâ fîsâcinâ, wa bârik lanâ fî muddinâ.
entries.translation: O Allah, bless us in our dates and bless us in our town, bless us in our Sa' and in our Mudd.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #187 · Muslim 2/1000 (Sa' and Mudd are both dry measures used for agricultural produce by the Arabs in the Prophet's time. Of the two, the So.' was the larger measure.) (Translator)

categories.name: Upon sneezing
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #188
entries.arabic: ((إِذَا عَطَسَ أَحَدُكُم فَلْيَقُلِ الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ، وَلْيَقُلْ لَهُ أَخُوهُ أَوْ صَاحِبُهُ: يَرْحَمُكَ اللَّهُ، فَإِذَا قَالَ لَهُ: يَرحَمُكَ اللَّهُ، فَلْيَقُلْ: يَهْدِيكُمُ اللَّهُ وَيُصْلِحُ بَالَكُمْ)).
entries.transliteration: - Alhamdu li-l-lâhi.- Yarhamuka l-lâhu.- Yahdîkumu l-lâhu wa yuslihu bâlakum.
entries.translation: When you sneeze, then say: All praises and thanks are to Allah. Your companion should say: May Allah have mercy upon you. When someone says Yarḥamukallāh to you then you should say: May Allah guide you and set your affairs in order.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #188 · Al-Bukhari 7/125.

categories.name: When a disbeliever praises Allah after sneezing
categories.arabicName: ما يقال للكافر إذا عطس فحمد الله
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #189
entries.arabic: ((يَهْدِيكُمُ اللَّهُ وَيُصْلِحُ بَالَكُمْ)).
entries.transliteration: Yahdîkumu l-lâhu wa yuslihu bâlakum.
entries.translation: May Allah guide you and set your affairs in order
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #189 · At-Tirmidhi 5/82, Ahmad 4/400, Abu Dawud 4/ 308. See also Al-Albani, Sahih At-Tirmidhi 2/354.

categories.name: To the newlywed
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #190
entries.arabic: ((بَارَكَ اللَّهُ لَكَ، وَبَارَكَ عَلَيْكَ، وَجَمَعَ بَيْنَكُمَا فِي خَيْرٍ)).
entries.transliteration: Bâraka l-lâhu laka, wa bârakacalayka, wa jamaca baynakumâ fî khayrin.
entries.translation: May Allah bless you, and shower His blessings upon you, and join you together in goodness.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #190 · Abu Dawud, Ibn Majah and At-Tirmidhi. See also Al-Albani, Sahih At-Tirmidhi 1/316.

categories.name: On the wedding night or when buying an animal
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #191
entries.arabic: إِذَا تَزَوَّجَ أَحَدُكُمُ امْرَأَةً، أَوْ إِذَا اشْتَرَى خَادِماً فَلْيَقُلْ: ((اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ خَيْرَهَا، وَخَيْرَ مَا جَبَلْتَهَا عَلَيْهِ، وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّهَا، وَشَرِّ مَا جَبَلْتَهَا عَلَيْهِ، وَإِذَا اشْتَرَى بَعِيراً فَلْيَأْخُذْ بِذِرْوَةِ سَنَامِهِ وَلْيَقُلْ مِثْلَ ذَلِكَ)).
entries.transliteration: Allâhumma innî as'aluka khayrahâ, wa khayra mâ jabaltahâcalayhi. Wa acûdhu bika min sharrihâ wa sharri mâ jabaltahâcalayhi.
entries.translation: When any of you marries a woman or purchases a maid-servant then let him say : O Allah, I ask You for the goodness of her, and the goodness upon which You have created her, and I seek refuge in You from the evil of her, and from the evil upon which You have created her. If you purchase a camel then take hold of the top of its hump and say the same.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #191 · Abu Dawud 2/248 and Ibn Majah 1/617. See also Al-Albani, Sahih Ibn Majah 1/324.

categories.name: Before sexual intercourse with the wife
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #192
entries.arabic: ((بِسْمِ اللَّهِ، اللَّهُمَّ جَنِّبْنَا الشَّيْطَانَ، وَجَنِّبِ الشَّيْطَانَ مَا رَزَقْتَنَا)).
entries.transliteration: Bismi l-lâhi. Allâhumma jannibnâ sh-shaytâna, wa jannibi sh-shaytâna mâ razaqtanâ.
entries.translation: With the Name of Allah. O Allah, keep the Devil away from us, and keep the Devil away from that which You provide for us.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #192 · Al-Bukhari 6/141, Muslim 2/1028.
