categories.name: When angry
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #193
entries.arabic: ((أَعُوذُ بِاللَّهِ مِنَ الشَّيْطَانِ الرَّجِيمِ)).
entries.transliteration: Acûdhu bi-l-lâhi mina sh-shaytâni r-rajîm.
entries.translation: I seek refuge in Allah from Satan the outcast.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #193 · Al-Bukhari 7/99, Muslim 4/2015.

categories.name: Upon seeing someone in trial or tribulation
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #194
entries.arabic: ((الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي عَافَانِي مِمَّا ابْتَلاَكَ بِهِ، وَفَضَّلَنِي عَلَى كَثِيرٍ مِمَّنْ خَلَقَ تَفْضِيلاً)).
entries.transliteration: Alhamdu li-l-lâhi l-ladhîcâfânî mimmâ btalâka bihi wa faddalanîcalâ kathîrin mimman khalaqa tafdîlan.
entries.translation: Praise is to Allah Who has spared me what He has afflicted you with, and preferred me greatly above much of what He has created.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #194 · At-Tirmidhi 5/493,4. See also Al-Albani, Sahih At-Tirmidhi 3/153.

categories.name: At a sitting or gathering
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #195
entries.arabic: ((عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ رضي الله عنه قَاَلَ: كَانَ يُعَدُّ لِرَسُولِ اللَّهِ صلى الله عليه وسلم فِي الْمَجْلِسِ الوَاحِدِ مِائَةُ مَرَّةٍ مِنْ قَبْلِ أَنْ يَقُومَ: ((رَبِّ اغْفِرْ لِي، وَتُبْ عَلَيَّ، إِنَّكَ أَنْتَ التَّوَّابُ الغَفُورُ)).
entries.transliteration: Rabbi ghfir lî wa tubcalayya. Innaka anta t-tawwâbu-l-ghafûr.
entries.translation: Ibn Umar (ra) said: Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) used to repeat in a single sitting: My Lord, forgive me, and accept my repentance, You are the Ever-Relenting, the All-Forgiving.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #195 · Sahih Ibn Majah 2/321. See also Al-Albani, Sahih At-Tirmidhi 3/153.

categories.name: For the expiation of sins, said at the conclusion of a sitting or gathering
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #196
entries.arabic: ((سُبْحَانَكَ اللَّهُمَّ وَبِحَمْدِكَ، أَشْهَدُ أَنْ لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ أَنْتَ، أَسْتَغْفِرُكَ وَأَتُوبُ إِلَيْكَ)).
entries.transliteration: Subhânaka l-lâhumma wa bi-hamdika. Ash-hadu an lâ ilâha illâ anta, astaghfiruka wa atûbu ilayka.
entries.translation: Glory is to You, O Allah, and praise is to You. I bear witness that there is none worthy of worship but You. I seek Your forgiveness and repent to You.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #196 · Abu Dawud, Ibn Majah, At-Tirmidhi and An-Nasa'i. See also Al-Albani, Sahih At-Tirmidhi 3/ 153. Aishah (RA) said: "Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) did not sit in a gathering, and did not recite the Qur'an, and did not perform any prayer without concluding by saying ... (then she quoted the above)." This was reported by An-Nasa'i in 'Amalul-Yawm wal-Laylah (no.308), and Dr. Farooq Hamadah graded it authentic in his checking of the same book, p. 273. See also Ahmad 6/77

categories.name: Returning a supplication of forgiveness
categories.arabicName: الدعاء لمن قال غفر الله لك
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #197
entries.transliteration: Wa laka.
entries.translation: And you.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #197 · Ahmad 5/82, and An-Nasa'i in 'Amalul-Yawm wal-Laylah p. 218, with checking by Dr. Farooq Hamadah.

categories.name: To one who does you a favour
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #198
entries.arabic: ((جَزَاكَ اللَّهُ خَيْراً)).
entries.transliteration: Jazâka l-lâhu khayran.
entries.translation: May Allah reward you with good.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #198 · At-Tirmidhi (no. 2035). See also Al-Albani, Sahih At-Tirmidhi 2/200 and Sahihul-Jami' (no. 6244).

categories.name: Protection from the Dajjal
categories.arabicName: ما يعصم الله به من الدجال
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #199
entries.arabic: ((مَنْ حَفِظَ عَشْرَ آيَاتٍ مِنْ أَوَّلِ سُورَةِ الْكَهْفِ عُصِمَ مِنَ الدَّجَّالِ)) ، وَالْاسْتِعَاذَةُ بِاللَّهِ مِنْ فِتْنَتِهِ عَقِبَ التَّشَهُّدِ الْأَخِيرِ مِنْ كُلِّ صَلاَةٍ.
entries.transliteration: - Voir invocations n°55 et 56
entries.translation: Whoever memorizes ten 'Ayat (Verses) from the beginning of Surat Al-Kahf, will be protected from the False Messiah 1 if he recites in every prayer after the final Tashahhud before ending the prayer, seeking the protection of Allah from the trials of the False Messiah.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #199 · 1 Muslim 1/555, another version mentions the last ten 'Ayat, Muslim 1/556. 2 See invocations no. 55 and 56 of this book.

categories.name: To one who pronounces his love for you, for Allah's sake
categories.arabicName: الدعاء لمن قال إني أحبك في الله
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #200
entries.arabic: ((أَحَبَّكَ الَّذِي أَحْبَبْتَنِي لَهُ)).
entries.transliteration: Ahabbaka l-ladhî ahbabtanî lahu.
entries.translation: May He for Whose sake you love me, love you.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #200 · Abu Dawud 4/333. Al-Albani graded it good in Sahih Abu Dawud 3/965.

categories.name: To one who has offered you some of his wealth
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #201
entries.arabic: ((بَارَكَ اللَّهُ لَكَ فِي أَهْلِكَ وَمَالِكَ)).
entries.transliteration: Bâraka l-lâhu laka fî ahlika wa mâlika.
entries.translation: May Allah bless you in your family and your property.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #201 · Al-Bukhari, cf. Al-Asqalani, Fathul-BAri 4/88.

categories.name: To the debtor when his debt is settled
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #202
entries.arabic: ((بارَكَ اللَّهُ لَكَ فِي أَهْلِكَ وَمَالِكَ، إِنَّمَا جَزَاءُ السَّلَفِ الْحَمْدُ وَالأَدَاءُ)).
entries.transliteration: Bâraka l-lâhu laka fî ahlika wa mâlika. Innamâ jazâ'u s-salafi-l-hamdu wa-l-adâ'.
entries.translation: May Allah bless you in your family and your wealth, surely the reward for a loan is praise and returning (what was borrowed).
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #202 · An-Nasa'i, 'Amalul-Yawm wal-Laylahp. 300, Ibn Majah 2/809. See also Al-Albani, Sahih Ibn Majah2/55.

categories.name: For fear of shirk
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #203
entries.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ أَنْ أُشْرِكَ بِكَ وَأَنَا أَعْلَمُ، وَأَسْتَغْفِرُكَ لِمَا لاَ أَعْلَمُ)).
entries.transliteration: Allâhumma innî acûdhu bika an ushrika bika wa anâ aclamu, wa astaghfiruka li-mâ lâ aclam.
entries.translation: O Allah, I seek refuge in You lest I associate anything with You knowingly, and I seek Your forgiveness for what I know not.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #203 · Ahmad 4/403. See also Al-Albani, Sahihul-Jami' As-Saghir 3/233 and Sahihut-Targhib wat- Tarhib 1/19.

categories.name: To someone who says "May Allah bless you"
categories.arabicName: الدعاء لمن قال بارك الله فيك
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #204
entries.arabic: ((وَفِيكَ بَارَكَ اللَّهُ)).
entries.transliteration: Wa fîka bârakallah
entries.translation: And may Allah bless you.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #204 · Ibn As-Sunni, p. 138, (no. 278). See also Ibn Al-Qayyim, Al-Wdbil As-Sayyib, p. 304, with checking by Basheer Muhammad ?Uyoon.

categories.name: The scorn of ascribing things to evil omens
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #205
entries.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ لاَ طَيْرَ إِلاَّ طَيْرُكَ، وَلاَ خَيْرَ إِلاَّ خَيْرُكَ، وَلاَ إِلَهَ غَيْرُكَ)).
entries.transliteration: Allâhumma, lâtayra illâtayruka, wa lâ khayra illâ khayruka, wa lâ ilâha ghayruka.
entries.translation: O Allah there is no portent other than Your portent, no goodness other than Your goodness, and none worthy of worship other than You.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #205 · Ahmad 2/220, Ibn As-Sunni (no. 292). See also Al-Albani, Silsilatul-'Ahadlth As-Sahihah 3/54, (no. 1065). As for bodings of good, these used to please the Prophet (ﷺ) and so when he heard good words from someone, he used to say: "We have taken from you a good portent from your mouth," Abu Dawud, Ahmad. See also Al-Albani, Silsilatul-'Ahadith As-Sahihah 2/363, and it is with Abu Ash-Shaikh Al-Asfahani in 'Akhlaqun-Nabiyy, pg. 270.

categories.name: When mounting an animal or any means of transport
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #206
entries.arabic: ((بِسْمِ اللَّهِ، وَالْحَمْدُ للَّهِ ﴿سُبْحَانَ الَّذِي سَخَّرَ لَنَا هَذَا وَمَا كُنَّا لَهُ مُقْرِنِينَ، وَإِنَّا إِلَى رَبِّنَا لَمُنقَلِبُونَ﴾، ((الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ، الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ، الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ، اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، سُبْحَانَكَ اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي ظَلَمْتُ نَفْسِي فَاغْفِرْ لِي؛ فَإِنَّهُ لاَ يَغْفِرُ الذُّنُوبَ إِلاَّ أَنْتَ)).
entries.transliteration: Bismi l-lâhi. Alhamdu li-l-lâhi. Subhâna l-ladhî sakhkhara lanâ hâdhâ wa mâ kunnâ lahu muqrinîn, wa innâ ilâ rabbinâ la-munqalibûn. Alhamdu li-l-lâhi, alhamdu li-l-lâhi, alhamdu li-l-lâhi. Allâhu akbaru, Allâhu akbaru, Allâhu akbaru. Subhânaka l-lâhumma innîzalamtu nafsî, fa-ghfir lî, fa-innahu lâ yaghfiru dh-dhunûba illâ ant.
entries.translation: With the Name of Allah. Praise is to Allah. Glory is to Him Who has provided this for us though we could never have had it by our efforts. Surely, unto our Lord, we are returning. Praise is to Allah. Praise is to Allah. Praise is to Allah. Allah is the Most Great. Allah is the Most Great. Allah is the Most Great. Glory is to You. O Allah, I have wronged my own soul. Forgive me, for surely none forgives sins but You.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #206 · Abu Dawud 3/34, At-Tirmidhi 5/501. See also Al-Albani, Sahih At-Tirmidhi3/156.

categories.name: For travel
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #207
entries.arabic: اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، ﴿سُبْحَانَ الَّذِي سَخَّرَ لَنَا هَذَا وَمَا كُنَّا لَهُ مُقْرِنِينَ * وَإِنَّا إِلَى رَبِّنَا لَمُنقَلِبُونَ﴾ ((اللَّهُمَّ إِنّا نَسْأَلُكَ فِي سَفَرِنَا هَذَا البِرَّ وَالتَّقْوَى، وَمِنَ الْعَمَلِ مَا تَرْضَى، اللَّهُمَّ هَوِّنْ عَلَيْنَا سَفَرَنَا هَذَا وَاطْوِ عَنَّا بُعْدَهُ، اللَّهُمَّ أَنْتَ الصَّاحِبُ فِي السَّفَرِ، وَالْخَليفَةُ فِي الْأَهْلِ، اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ وَعْثَاءِ السَّفَرِ، وَكَآبَةِ الْمَنْظَرِ، وَسُوءِ الْمُنْقَلَبِ فِي الْمَالِ وَالْأَهْلِ))، وإذا رَجَعَ قَالَهُنَّ وَزَادَ فِيهِنَّ: ((آيِبُونَ، تائِبُونَ، عَابِدُونَ، لِرَبِّنَا حَامِدُونَ)).
entries.transliteration: Allâhu akbaru, Allâhu akbaru, Allâhu akbaru. Subhâna l-ladhî sakhkhara lanâ hâdhâ wa mâ kunnâ lahu muqrinîn, wa innâ ilâ rabbinâ la-munqalibûn. Allâhumma innâ nas-aluka fî safarinâ hâdhâ al-birra wa t-taqwâ wa mina-l-camali mâ tardâ. Allâhumma hawwincalayna safaranâ hâdhâ watwicannâ bucdahu. Allâhumma antas-sâhibu fî s-safari, wa-l-khalîfatu fî-l-ahli. Allâhumma innî acûdhu bika min wacthâ'i s-safari wa ka'âbati-l-manzari, wa sû'i-l-munqalabi fî-lmâli wa-l-ahli.Au retour, répéter l'invocation et ajouter :Âyibûn, tâ'ibûn,câbidûn, li-rabbinâhâmidûn.
entries.translation: Allah is the Most Great. Allah is the Most Great. Allah is the Most Great. Glory is to Him Who has provided this for us though we could never have had it by our efforts. Surely, unto our Lord we are returning. O Allah, we ask You on this our journey for goodness and piety, and for works that are pleasing to You. O Allah, lighten this journey for us and make its distance easy for us. O Allah, You are our Companion on the road and the One in Whose care we leave our family. O Allah, I seek refuge in You from this journey's hardships, and from the wicked sights in store and from finding our family and property in misfortune upon returning. (Upon returning recite the same again adding :) We return repentant to our Lord, worshipping our Lord, and praising our Lord.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #207 · Muslim 2/978.

categories.name: Upon entering a town or village
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #208
entries.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ رَبَّ السَّمَوَاتِ السَّبْعِ وَمَا أَظْلَلْنَ، وَرَبَّ الأَرَضِينَ السَّبْعِ وَمَا أَقْلَلْنَ، وَرَبَّ الشَّياطِينِ وَمَا أَضْلَلْنَ، وَرَبَّ الرِّيَاحِ وَمَا ذَرَيْنَ، أَسْأَلُكَ خَيْرَ هَذِهِ الْقَرْيَةِ، وَخَيْرَ أَهْلِهَا، وَخَيْرَ مَا فِيهَا، وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّهَا، وَشَرِّ أَهْلِهَا، وَشَرِّ مَا فِيهَا)).
entries.transliteration: Allâhumma rabba s-samâwâti s-sabci wa mâ zzlalna, wa rabba-l-aradîna s-sabci wa mâ aqlalna, wa rabba sh-shayâtîni wa mâ adlalna, wa rabba r-riyâhi wa mâ dharayna. As'alukakhayra hâdhihi-l-qaryati wa khayra ahlihâ wa khayra mâ fîhâ, wa acûdhu bika min sharrihâ wa sharri ahlihâ wa sharri mâ fîhâ.
entries.translation: O Allah, Lord of the seven heavens and all they overshadow, Lord of the seven worlds and all they uphold, Lord of the devils and all they lead astray, Lord of the winds and all they scatter. I ask You for the goodness of this town, and for the goodness of its people, and for the goodness it contains. I seek refuge in You from its evil, from the evil of its people, and from the evil it contains.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #208 · Al-Hakim who graded it authentic and Ath-Tfaahabi agreed 2/100, and Ibn As-Sunni (no. 524). Al-Hafidh graded it good in his checking of Al-'Athkdr 5/154. Ibn Baz said in Tuhfatul-'Akhydr p. 37, that An-Nasa'i recorded it with a good chain of narration.

categories.name: When entering the market
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #209
entries.arabic: ((لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لاَ شَرِيكَ لَهُ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ، وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ، يُحْيِي وَيُمِيتُ، وَهُوَ حَيٌّ لاِ يَمُوتُ، بِيَدِهِ الْخَيْرُ، وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ)).
entries.transliteration: Lâ ilâha illâ l-lâhu, wahdahu lâ sharîka lahu, lahu-l-mulku wa lahu-l-hamdu, yuhyî wa yumîtu, wa huwahayyun lâ yamûtu, bi-yadihi-l-khayru, wa huwacalâ kulli shay'in qadîr.
entries.translation: None has the right to be worshipped but Allah alone, Who has no partner. His is the dominion and His is the praise. He brings life and He causes death, and He is living and does not die. In His Hand is all good, and He is Able to do all things.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #209 · At-Tirmidhi 5/291, and Al-Hakim 1/538. Al-Albani graded it good in Sahih Ibn Majah 2/21 and Sahih At-Tirmidhi 3/152.

categories.name: When the mounted animal (or means of transport) stumbles
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #210
entries.transliteration: Bismillâh.
entries.translation: With the Name of Allah.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #210 · Abu Dawud 4/296. Al-Albani graded it authentic in Sahih Abu Dawud 3/941.

categories.name: Supplication of the traveller for the resident
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #211
entries.arabic: ((أَسْتَوْدِعُكُمُ اللَّهَ الَّذِي لاَ تَضِيعُ وَدَائِعُهُ)).
entries.transliteration: Astawdicukumu l-lâha l-ladhî lâ tadîcu wadâ'icuhu.
entries.translation: I place you in the trust of Allah, whose trust is never misplaced.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #211 · Ahmad 2/403, Ibn Majah 2/943. See also Al-Albani, Sahih Ibn Majah 2/133.

categories.name: Supplication of the resident for the traveller
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 2 invocations.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #212
entries.arabic: (( أَسْتَوْدِعُ اللَّهَ دِينَكَ، وَأَمَانَتَكَ، وَخَوَاتِيمَ عَمَلِكَ)).
entries.transliteration: Astawdicu l-lâha dînaka wa amânataka wa khawâtimacamalika.
entries.translation: I leave your religion in the care of Allah, as well as your safety, and the last of your deeds.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #212 · Ahmad 2/7, At-Tirmidhi 5/499. See also Al-Albani, Sahih At-Tirmidhi 2/155.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #213
entries.arabic: ((زَوَّدَكَ اللَّهُ التَّقْوَى، وَغَفَرَ ذَنْبَكَ، وَيَسَّرَ لَكَ الخَيْرَ حَيْثُ ما كُنْتَ)).
entries.transliteration: Zawwadaka l-lâhu t-taqwâ, wa ghafara dhanbaka, wa yassara laka-l-khayrahaythu mâ kunta.
entries.translation: May Allah give you piety as your provision, forgive your sins, and make goodness easy for you wherever you are.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #213 · At-Tirmidhi. See Al-Albani, Sahih At-Tirmidhi, 3/155.

categories.name: Takbir and Tasbih during travel
categories.arabicName: التكبير و التسبيح في سير السفر
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #214
entries.arabic: قَالَ جَابِرٌ رضي الله عنه: ((كُنَّا إِذَا صَعَدْنَا كَبَّرْنَا، وَإِذَا نَزَلْنَا سَبَّحْنَا)).
entries.transliteration: Lors d'une montée : Allâhu akbar.Lors d'une descente : Subhânallâh.
entries.translation: Jabir (ra), said: Whenever we went up a hill we would say Allāhu Akbar (Allah is the Most Great) and when we descended we would say Subḥānallāh (Glory is to Allah).
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #214 · Al-Bukhari, cf. Al-Asqalani, Fathul-Bari 6/135.

categories.name: Prayer of the traveller as dawn approaches
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #215
entries.arabic: ((سَمَّعَ سَامِعٌ بِحَمْدِ اللَّهِ، وَحُسْنِ بَلاَئِهِ عَلَيْنَا، رَبَّنَا صاحِبْنَا، وَأَفْضِلْ عَلَيْنَا، عَائِذاً بِاللَّهِ مِنَ النَّارِ)).
entries.transliteration: Sammaca sâmicun bi-hamdi l-lâhi, wahusni balâ'ihicalayna. Rabbanâsâhibnâ wa afdilcalaynâcâ'idhan bi-l-lâhi mina n-nâr.
entries.translation: He Who listens has heard that we praise Allah for the good things He gives us. Our Lord, be with us and bestow Your favor upon us. I seek the protection of Allah from the Fire.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #215 · Muslim 4/2086, the meaning of sami`a sāmi`un (who listens has heard) is that 'a witness has witnessed our praise of Allah due to His blessings and favor upon us.' It could also be read samma`a sāmi`un, in which case it means 'one who has heard this statement of mine will convey it to another and he will say it as well.' This is due to the attention given to the Thikr (remembrance of Allah) and supplications made during the early morning hours. The meaning of his saying 'Our Lord, be with us and bestow Your favor upon us' is: 'Our Lord, protect us and guard us. Bless us with Your numerous bounties, and avert from us every evil.' See An-Nawawi, Sharh Sahih Muslim 17/39.

categories.name: Stopping or lodging somewhere in travel and otherwise
categories.arabicName: الدعاء إذا نزل مترلا في سفر أو غيره
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #216
entries.arabic: ((أَعُوذُ بِكَلِمَاتِ اللَّهِ التَّامَّاتِ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ)).
entries.transliteration: Acûdhu bi-kalimâti l-lâhi t-tâmmâti min sharri mâ khalaq.
entries.translation: I seek refuge in the Perfect Words of Allah from the evil of what He has created.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #216 · Muslim 4/2080.

categories.name: While returning from travel
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #217
entries.arabic: ((يُكَبِّرُ عَلَى كُلِّ شَرَفٍ ثَلاَثَ تَكْبِيرَاتٍ ثُمَّ يَقُولُ: لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لاَ شَرِيكَ لَهُ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ، وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ، وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ، آيِبُونَ، تَائِبُونَ، عَابِدُونَ، لِرَبِّنا حَامِدُونَ، صَدَقَ اللَّهُ وَعْدَهُ، وَنَصَرَ عَبْدَهُ، وَهَزَمَ الْأَحْزابَ وَحْدَهُ)).
entries.transliteration: Chaque fois que l'on atteint un point élevé : Allâhu akbar (trois fois).Dire ensuite : Lâ ilâha illâ l-lâhu, wahdahu lâ sharîka lahu, lahu-l-mulku wa lahu-l-hamdu, wa huwacalâ kulli shay'in qadîrun. Âyibûna, tâ'ibûna,câbidûna, li-rabbinâhâmidûn.Sadaqa l-lâhu wacdahu, wa nasaracabdahu wa hazama-l-ahzâba wahdahu.
entries.translation: From every elevated point say Allāhu Akbar (Allah is the Most Great) three times and then recite: None has the right to be worshipped but Allah alone, Who has no partner. His is the dominion and His is the praise, and He is Able to do all things. We return repentant to our Lord, worshipping our Lord, and praising our Lord. He fulfilled His Promise, He aided His slave, and He alone defeated the Confederates.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #217 · Bukhari 7/163, Muslim 2/980. The Prophet (ﷺ) used to say this when returning from a campaign or from Hajj.

categories.name: What to say upon receiving pleasing or displeasing news
categories.arabicName: ما يقول من أتاه أمر يسره أو يكرهه
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #218
entries.arabic: كَانَ النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم إِذَا أَتَاهُ الْأَمْرُ يَسُرُّهُ قَالَ: ((الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي بِنِعْمَتِهِ تَتِمُّ الصَّالِحَاتُ)) وَإِذَا أَتَاهُ الْأَمْرُ يَكْرَهُهُ قَالَ: ((الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ عَلَى كُلِّ حَالٍ)).
entries.transliteration: Alhamdu li-l-lâhi l-ladhî bi-nicmatihi tatimmus-sâlihâtu.Alhamdu li-l-lâhicalâ kullihâlin.
entries.translation: When something happened that pleased him, the Prophet (ﷺ) used to say: Praise is to Allah Who by His blessings all good things are perfected. And if something happened that displeased him, he used to say: Praise is to Allah in all circumstances.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #218 · Ibn As-Sunni, 'Amalul-Yawm wal-Laylah, and Al-Hakim who graded it authentic 1/499. See also Al-Albani, Sahihul-Jami' As-Saghir 4/201.

categories.name: Excellence of sending prayers upon the Prophet (ﷺ)
categories.arabicName: فضل الصلاة على النبي صلى الله عليه و سلم
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 5 invocations.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #219
entries.arabic: قَالَ النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((مَنْ صَلَّى عَلَيَّ صَلاَةً صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ بِهَا عَشْراً)).
entries.transliteration: Allâhummasallicalâ Muhammadin wacalâ âli Muhammadin kamâsallaytacalâ Ibrâhîma wacalâ âli Ibrâhîma. Innakahamîdun, majîd. Allâhumma bârikcalâ Muhammadin wacalâ âli Muhammadin kamâ bâraktacalâ Ibrâhîma wacalâ âli Ibrâhîma. Innakahamîdun, majîd.
entries.translation: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: "Whoever prays for Allah's blessings upon me once, will be blessed for it by Allah ten times."
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #219 · Muslim 1/288.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #220
entries.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((لاَ تَجْعَلُوا قَبْرِي عِيداً وَصَلُّوا عَلَيَّ؛ فَإِنَّ صَلاَتَكُم تَبْلُغُنِي حَيْثُ كُنْتُمْ)).
entries.transliteration: Allâhummasallicalâ Muhammadin wacalâ âli Muhammadin kamâsallaytacalâ Ibrâhîma wacalâ âli Ibrâhîma. Innakahamîdun, majîd. Allâhumma bârikcalâ Muhammadin wacalâ âli Muhammadin kamâ bâraktacalâ Ibrâhîma wacalâ âli Ibrâhîma. Innakahamîdun, majîd.
entries.translation: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: "Do not make my grave a place of ritual celebration, but pray for Allah's blessings upon me, for your blessings reach me from wherever you are.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #220 · Abu Dawud 2/218, Ahmad 2/367. Al-Albani graded it authentic in Sahih Abu Dawud 2/383.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #221
entries.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((الْبَخِيلُ مَنْ ذُكِرْتُ عِنْدَهُ فَلَمْ يُصَلِّ عَلَيَّ)).
entries.transliteration: Salla l-lâhucalayhi wa sallam.
entries.translation: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: "The miser is the one in whose presence I am mentioned yet does not pray for Allah's blessings upon me."
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #221 · At-Tirmidhi 5/551 and others. See also Al-Albani, Sahih At-Tirmidhi 3/177 and Sahihul-Jarai' As-Saghir 3/25.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #222
entries.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم:((إِنَّ لِلَّهِ مَلاَئِكَةً سَيَّاحِينَ فِي الْأَرْضِ يُبَلِّغُونِي مِنْ أُمَّتِي السَّلاَمَ)).
entries.transliteration: Not applicable — this entry records Prophetic guidance rather than a separate recited formula.
entries.translation: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: "Indeed Allah has angels who roam the earth and they convey to me the greetings (or prayers of peace) of my Ummah (nation)."
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #222 · An-Nasa'i, Al-Hakim 2/421. Al-Albani graded it authentic in Sahih An-Nasa'i 1/274.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #223
entries.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((مَا مِنْ أَحَدٍ يُسَلِّمُ عَلَيَّ إِلاَّ رَدَّ اللَّهُ عَلَيَّ رُوحِيَ حَتَّى أَرُدَّ عَلَيْهِ السَّلاَمَ)).
entries.transliteration: Not applicable — this entry records Prophetic guidance rather than a separate recited formula.
entries.translation: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: "No one sends greetings (or prayers of peace) upon me but Allah returns my soul to me so that I may return his greetings."
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #223 · Abu Dawud (no. 2041). Al-Albani graded it good in Sahih Abu Dawud 1/383.

categories.name: Excellence of spreading the Islamic greeting
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 3 invocations.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #224
entries.arabic: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((لاَ تَدْخُلُوا الْجَنَّةَ حَتَّى تُؤْمِنُوا، وَلاَ تُؤْمِنُوا حَتَّى تَحَابُّوا، أَوَلاَ أَدُلُّكُم عَلَى شَيْءٍ إِذَا فَعَلْتُمُوهُ تَحَابَبْتُم، أَفْشُوا السَّلاَمَ بَيْنَكُمْ)).
entries.transliteration: As-salâmucalaykum.
entries.translation: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: "You shall not enter Paradise until you believe, and you have not believed until you love one another. Shall I tell you of something you can do to make you love one another? Spread the greetings of Salam (peace) amongst yourselves (i.e. between each other).
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #224 · Muslim 1/74 and others.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #225
entries.arabic: ((ثَلاَثٌ مَنْ جَمَعَهُنَّ فَقَدْ جَمَعَ الْإِيمَانَ: الْإِنْصَافُ مِنْ نَفْسِكَ، وَبَذْلُ السَّلاَمِ لِلْعَالَمِ، وَالْإِنْفَاقُ مِنَ الإِقْتَارِ)).
entries.transliteration: Not applicable — this entry records Prophetic guidance rather than a separate recited formula.
entries.translation: Three characteristics, whoever combines them, has completed his faith: to be just, to spread greetings to all people and to spend (charitably) out of the little you have.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #225 · Al-Bukhari, cf. Al-Asqalani, Fathul-Bari 1/82 as a statement of the Companion 'Ammar (RA).

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #226
entries.arabic: وَعَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُمَرَ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمَا: أنَّ رَجُلاً سَأَلَ النَّبِيَّ صلى الله عليه وسلم أيُّ الْإِسْلاَمِ خَيْرٌ قَالَ: ((تُطْعِمُ الطَّعَامَ، وَتَقْرأُ السَّلاَمَ عَلَى مَنْ عَرَفْتَ وَمَنْ لَمْ تَعْرِفْ)).
entries.transliteration: Not applicable — this entry records Prophetic guidance rather than a separate recited formula.
entries.translation: `Abdullah bin `Umar (RA) said: A man asked the Prophet (ﷺ), "What is the best act of Islam?" He said, "To feed others and to give greetings of Salam (peace) to those whom you know and to those whom you do not know. "
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #226 · Al-Bukhari, cf. Al-Asqalani, Fathul-Bari 1/55, Muslim 1/65.

categories.name: Returning a greeting to a disbeliever
categories.arabicName: كيف يرد السلام على الكافر إذا سلم
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #227
entries.arabic: ((إذَا سَلَّمَ عَلَيْكُمْ أَهْلُ الْكِتَابِ فَقُولُوا: وَعَلَيْكُمْ)).
entries.transliteration: Wacalaykum.
entries.translation: If one of the People of the Scripture (i.e. Christians and Jews) greets you saying As-Salaamu `alaykum, then say (to him): And upon you .
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #227 · Al-Bukhari, cf. Al-Asqalani, Fathul-Bari 11/42, Muslim 4/1705.

categories.name: Upon hearing a rooster crow or the braying of a donkey
categories.arabicName: الدُّعاءُ عِنْدَ سَمَاعِ صِياحِ الدِّيكِ ونَهِيقِ الْحِمَارِ
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #228
entries.arabic: ((إِذَا سَمِعْتُمْ صِيَاحَ الدِّيَكَةِ فَاسْأَلُوا اللَّهَ مِنْ فَضْلِهِ؛ فَإِنَّهَا رَأَتْ مَلَكاً وَإِذَا سَمِعْتُمْ نَهِيقَ الْحِمَارِ فَتَعَوَّذُوا بِاللَّهِ مِنَ الشَّيطَانِ؛ فَإِنَّهُ رَأَى شَيْطَاناً)).
entries.transliteration: Acûdhu bi-l-lâhi mina sh-shaytâni r-rajîm.
entries.translation: When you hear the cock's crow, ask Allah for His favor upon you for surely it has seen an angel. When you hear the bray of a donkey, seek refuge in Allah from Satan, for surely it has seen a devil.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #228 · Al-Bukhari, cf. Al-Asqalani, Fathul-Bari 6/350, Muslim 4/2092.

categories.name: Upon hearing the barking of dogs at night
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #229
entries.arabic: ((إِذَا سَمِعْتُمْ نُبَاحَ الْكِلاَبِ وَنَهِيقَ الْحَمِيرِ بِاللَّيْلِ فَتَعَوَّذُوا بِاللَّهِ مِنْهُنَّ؛ فَإِنَّهُنَّ يَرَيْنَ مَا لاَ تَرَوْنَ)).
entries.transliteration: Acûdhu bi-l-lâhi mina sh-shaytâni r-rajîm.
entries.translation: When you hear a dog barking or a donkey braying in the night, then seek refuge in Allah from them, for surely they have seen what you see not.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #229 · Abu Dawud 4/327, Ahmad 3/306. Al-Albani graded it authentic in Sahih Abu Dawud 3/961.

categories.name: For one you have insulted
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #230
entries.arabic: قَالَ النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((اللَّهُمَّ فَأَيُّمَا مُؤْمِنٍ سَبَبْتُهُ فَاجْعَلْ ذَلِكَ لَهُ قُرْبَةً إِلَيْكَ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ)).
entries.transliteration: Allâhumma fa-ayyumâ mu'minin sababtuhu, fa-jcal dhâlika lahu qurbatan ilayka yawma-l-qiyâmati.
entries.translation: O Allah, whomever of the believers I have abused, give him the reward of a sacrificial slaughter for it on the Day of Resurrection.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #230 · Al-Bukhari, cf. Al-Asqalani, Fathul-Bari 11/171, Muslim 4/2007. The wording in Muslim's report is: 'make it a purification for him and a source of mercy.

categories.name: The etiquette of praising a fellow Muslim
categories.arabicName: ما يقول المسلم إذا مدح المسلم
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #231
entries.arabic: قَالَ النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((إِذَا كَانَ أَحَدُكُم مَادِحاً صَاحِبَهُ لاَ مَحَالَةَ فَلْيَقُلْ: أَحْسِبُ فُلاَناً وَاللَّهُ حَسِيبُهُ، وَلاَ أُزَكِّي عَلَى اللَّهِ أَحَداً، أَحْسِبُهُ – إِنْ كَانَ يَعْلَمُ ذَاكَ – كَذَا وَكَذَا)).
entries.transliteration: Not applicable — this entry records Prophetic guidance rather than a separate recited formula.
entries.translation: If any of you praises his companion then let him say: I consider (such and such a person), and Allah is his Assessor, (meaning: and I cannot claim anyone to be pious before Allah) if you know of this (good character trait in the person) to be such and such (saying what he thinks is praiseworthy in that person).
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #231 · Muslim 4/2296.

categories.name: For the one that have been praised
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #232
entries.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ لاَ تُؤَاخِذْنِي بِمَا يَقُولُونَ، وَاغْفِرْ لِي مَا لاَ يَعْلَمُونَ، [وَاجْعَلْنِي خَيْراً مِمَّا يَظُّنُّونَ])).
entries.transliteration: Allâhumma lâ tu'âkhidhnî bi-mâ yaqûlûna, wa ghfir lî mâ lâ yaclamun, [wa jcalnî khayran mimmâ yazunnûn].
entries.translation: O Allah, do not call me to account for what they say and forgive me for what they have no knowledge of [and make me better than they imagine].
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #232 · Al-Bukhari, Al-'Adabul-Mufrad no. 761. See Al-Albani, Sahih Al-'Adabul-Mufrad (no. 585). The portion between brackets if from Al-Bayhaqi, Shu'ab Al-Iman 4/228, and comes another account.

categories.name: The Talbiyah for Hajj or 'Umrah
categories.arabicName: كيف يلبي المحرم في الحج أو العمرة ؟
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #233
entries.arabic: ((لَبَّيْكَ اللَّهُمَّ لَبَّيْكَ، لَبَّيْكَ لاَ شَرِيكَ لَكَ لَبَّيْكَ، إِنَّ الْحَمْدَ، وَالنِّعْمَةَ، لَكَ وَالْمُلْكَ، لاَ شَرِيكَ لَكَ)).
entries.transliteration: Labbayka l-lâhumma labbayka. Lâ sharîka laka labbayka. Inna-l-hamda wa n-nicmata laka wa-l-mulku. Lâ sharîka laka.
entries.translation: Here I am O Allah, (in response to Your call), here I am. Here I am, You have no partner, here I am. Verily all praise, grace and sovereignty belong to You. You have no partner.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #233

categories.name: The Takbîr passing the black stone
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #234
entries.arabic: ((طَافَ النَّبيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم بِالْبَيْتِ عَلَى بَعِيرٍ كُلَّمَا أَتَى الرُّكْنَ أَشَارَ إِلَيْهِ بِشَيْءٍ عِنْدَهُ وَكَبَّرَ)).
entries.transliteration: Allâhu akbar.
entries.translation: The Prophet (ﷺ) performed Tawaf riding a camel. Every time he passed the corner (containing the Black Stone), he would point to it with something that he was holding and say: Allāhu Akbar (Allah is the Most Great)!
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #234 · Al-Bukhari, cf. Al-Asqalani, Fathul-Bari 3/476. See also 472. The 'something' that was referred to in this Hadith was a cane.

categories.name: Between the Yemeni corner and the black stone
categories.arabicName: الدعاء بين الركن اليماني والحجر الأسود
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #235
entries.arabic: ((﴿رَبَّنَا آتِنَا فِي الدُّنْيَا حَسَنَةً وَفِي الْآخِرَةِ حَسَنَةً وَقِنَا عَذَابَ النَّارِ﴾)).
entries.transliteration: Rabbanâ âtinâ fî d-dunyahasanatan wa fî-l-âkhiratihasanatan wa qinâcadhâba n-nâr.
entries.translation: Our Lord, grant us the good things in this world, and the good things in the next life, and save us from the punishment of the Fire.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #235 · Abu Dawud 2/179, Ahmad 3/411, Al-Baghawi, Sharhus-Sunnah 7/128. Al-Albani graded it good in Sahih Abu Dawud 1/354. The Ayat is from Surat Al-Baqarah, 2:201.

categories.name: When at Mount Safa and Mount Marwah
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #236
entries.arabic: لَمَّا دَنَا النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم مِنَ الصَّفَا قَرَأَ: ﴿إِنَّ الصَّفَا وَالْمَرْوَةَ مِنْ شَعَآئِرِ اللَّهِ﴾ أَبْدَأُ بِمَا بَدَأَ اللَّهُ بِهِ)) فَبَدَأَ بِالصَّفَا فَرَقِيَ عَلَيْهِ حَتَّى رَأَى الْبَيْتَ، فَاسْتَقْبَلَ الْقِبْلَةَ، فَوَحَّدَ اللَّهَ وَكبَّرَهُ وَقَالَ: ((لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لاَ شَرِيكَ لَهُ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ، لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ، أَنْجَزَ وَعْدَهُ، وَنَصَرَ عَبْدَهُ، وَهَزَمَ الْأَحْزَابَ وَحْدَهُ، ثُمَّ دَعَا بَيْنَ ذلكَ. قَالَ مِثْلَ هَذَا ثَلاَثَ مَرَّاتٍ)) الْحَدِيثُ. وَفِيهِ: ((فَفَعَلَ عَلَى الْمَرْوَةِ كَمَا فَعَلَ عَلَى الصَّفَا)).
entries.transliteration: VersSafâ : Innas-safâ wa-l-marwata min shacâ'iri l-lâh. Abda'u bimâ badaa l-lâhu bihi.Vers la Kacba : Lâ ilâha illâ l-lâhu, Allâhu akbar, Lâ ilâha illâ l-lâhu, wahdahu lâ sharîka lahu, lahu-l-mulku wa lahu-l-hamdu, wa huwacalâ kulli shay'in qadîrun. Lâ ilâha illâ l-lâhu wahdahu. Anjaza wacdahu, wa nasaracabdahu, wa hamaza-l-ahzâba wahdahu.
entries.translation: Whenever the Prophet (ﷺ) approached Mount Safa, he would recite: Surely Safa and Marwah are among the signs of Allah. I begin by that which Allah began. He began (his Sa'y) at Mount Safa climbing it until he could see the House. He then faced the Qiblah repeating the words: There is none worthy of worship but Allah, and Allah is the Most Great. Then he said: None has the right to be worshipped but Allah alone, Who has no partner, His is the dominion and His is the praise, and He is Able to do all things. None has the right to be worshipped but Allah alone, He fulfilled His Promise, He aided His slave, and He alone defeated Confederates. Then he would ask Allah for what he liked, repeating the same thing like this three times. He did at Mount Marwah as he did at Mount Safa.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #236 · Muslim 2/888.

categories.name: The Day of 'Arafah
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #237
entries.arabic: قَالَ النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((خَيْرُ الدُّعَاءِ دُعَاءُ يَوْمِ عَرَفَةَ، وَخَيْرُ مَا قُلْتُ أَنَا وَالنَّبيُّونَ مِنْ قَبْلِي: لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لاَ شَرِيكَ لَهُ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ)).
entries.transliteration: Lâ ilâha illâ l-lâhu wahdahu lâ sharîka lahu, lahu-l-mulku wa lahu lhamdu, wa huwacalâ kulli shay'in qadîr.
entries.translation: None has the right to be worshipped but Allah Alone, Who has no partner. His is the dominion and His is the praise, and He is Able to do all things.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #237 · At-Tirmidhi. Al-Albani graded it good in Sahih At-Tirmidhi 3/184, and also Silsilatul-'Ahadith As-Sahihah 4/6.

categories.name: Remembrance at Muzdalifa
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #238
entries.arabic: رَكِبَ النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم الْقَصْوَاءَ حَتَّى أَتَى الْمَشْعَرَ الْحَرَامَ فَاسْتَقْبَلَ الْقِبْلَةَ (فَدَعَاهُ، وَكَبَّرَهُ، وَهَللَّهُ، وَوَحَّدَهُ) فَلَمْ يَزَلْ وَاقِفاً حَتَّى أَسْفَرَ جِدَّاً فَدَفَعَ قَبْلَ أَنْ تَطْلُعَ الشَّمسُ.
entries.transliteration: Not applicable — this entry records Prophetic guidance rather than a separate recited formula.
entries.translation: 'He (ﷺ) rode Al-Qaswa until he reached Al-MashAAar Al-Haram, he then faced the qiblah, supplicated to Allah, and extoled His greatness and oneness. He stood until the sun shone but left before it rose.'
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #238 · Muslim 2/891.

categories.name: Takbir when throwing each pebble at the Jamarat
categories.arabicName: التكبير عند رمي الجمار مع كل حصاة
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #239
entries.arabic: ((يُكَبِّرُ كُلَّمَا رَمَى بِحَصَاةٍ عِنْدَ الْجِمَارِ الثَّلاَثِ، ثُمَّ يَتَقَدَّمُ، ويَقِفُ يَدْعُو مُسْتَقْبِلَ الْقِبلَةِ، رَافِعاً يَدَيْهِ بَعْدَ الْجَمْرَةِ الْأُولَى وَالثَّانِيَةِ. أَمَّا جَمْرَةُ الْعَقَبَةِ فَيَرْمِيهَا وَيُكَبِّرُ عِنْدَ كُلِّ حَصَاةٍ وَيَنْصَرِفُ وَلاَ يَقِفُ عِنْدَهَا)).
entries.transliteration: Allâhu akbar.
entries.translation: The Prophet (ﷺ) said Allāhu Akbar (Allah is the Most Great) with each pebble he threw at the three pillars. Then he went forward, stood facing the Qiblah, and raised his hands and called upon Allah. That was after (stoning) the first and second pillars. As for the third, he stoned it and called out Allāhu Akbar with every pebble he threw, but when he was finished he left without standing at it (for supplications).
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #239 · Al-Bukhari, cf. Al-Asqalani, Fathul-Bari 3/581, 3, 4, and Muslim

categories.name: At times of amazement and that which delights
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 2 invocations.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #240
entries.arabic: ((سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ!)).
entries.transliteration: Subhâna l-lâh.
entries.translation: (Glory is to Allah).
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #240 · Al-Bukhari, cf. Al-Asqalani, Fathul-Bari 1/210, 390, 414 and Muslim 4/1857.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #241
entries.transliteration: Allâhu akbar.
entries.translation: (Allah is the Most Great)
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #241 · Al-Bukhari, cf. Al-Asqalani, Fathul-Bari 8/441. See also Al-Albani, Sahih At-Tirmidhi 2/103, 235, Ahmad 5/218.

categories.name: Upon receiving pleasant news
categories.arabicName: ما يفعل من أتاه أمر يسره
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #242
entries.arabic: ((كَانَ النَّبيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم إِذَا أَتَاهُ أَمْرٌ يَسُرُّهُ أَوْ يُسَرُّ بِهِ خَرَّ سَاجِداً شُكْراً لِلَّهِ تَبَارَكَ وَتَعَالَى)).
entries.transliteration: Not applicable — this entry records Prophetic guidance rather than a separate recited formula.
entries.translation: The Prophet (ﷺ), upon receiving news which pleased him or which caused pleasure, would prostrate in gratitude to Allah blessed and exalted.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #242 · Abu Dawud, Ibn Majah, At-Tirmidhi. See also Al-Albani, Sahih Ibn Majah 1/233, and 'Irwa'ul-Ghalil 2/226.

categories.name: When feeling some pain in the body
categories.arabicName: ما يقول من أحس وجعا في جسده
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #243
entries.arabic: ((ضَعْ يَدَكَ عَلَى الَّذِي تَألَّمَ مِنْ جَسَدِكَ وَقُلْ: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ، ثَلاَثاً، وَقُلْ سَبْعَ مَرَّاتٍ: أَعُوذُ بِاللَّهِ وَقُدْرَتِهِ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا أَجِدُ وَأُحَاذِرُ)).
entries.transliteration: Bismi l-lâhi , puis : Acûdhu bi-l-lâhi wa qudratihi min sharri mâ ajidu wa uhâdhiru .
entries.translation: Put your hand on the place where you feel pain and say: With the Name of Allah (three times). Then say seven times: I seek refuge in Allah and in His Power from the evil of what I find and of what I guard against.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #243 · Muslim 4/1728.

categories.name: When in fear of afflicting something with an (evil) eye from oneself
categories.arabicName: دعاء من خشي أن يصيب شيئا بعينه
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #244
entries.arabic: ((إِذَا رَأَى أَحَدُكُم مِنْ أَخِيهِ، أَوْ مِنْ نَفْسِهِ، أَوْ مِنْ مَالِهِ مَا يُعْجِبُهُ [فَلْيَدْعُ لَهُ بِالْبَرَكَةِ] فَإِنَّ الْعَيْنَ حَقٌّ)).
entries.transliteration: Allâhumma bârikcalayhi.
entries.translation: If you see something from your brother, yourself or wealth which you find impressing, then invoke blessings for it, for the evil eye is indeed true.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #244 · Ahmad 4/447, Ibn Majah, Malik. Al-Albani graded it authentic in Sahihul-Jami' As-Saghir 1/212. Also see Al-Arna'ut's checking of Ibn Al-Qayyim's Zadul-Ma'ad 4/170.

categories.name: When startled
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #245
entries.arabic: ((لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ!)).
entries.transliteration: Lâ ilâha illâ l-lâhu.
entries.translation: There is none worthy of worship but Allah!
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #245 · Al-Bukhari, cf. Al-Asqalani, Fathul-Bari 6/181, Muslim 4/2208.

categories.name: When slaughtering or offering a sacrifice
categories.arabicName: ما يقول عند الذبح أو النحر
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #246
entries.arabic: ((بِسْمِ اللَّهِ وَاللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ [اللَّهُمَّ مِنْكَ وَلَكَ] اللَّهُمَّ تَقَبَّلْ مِنِّي)).
entries.transliteration: Bismi l-lâhi wa l-lâhu akbar. [Allâhumma minka wa laka]. Allâhumma taqabbal minnî.
entries.translation: With the Name of Allah, Allah is the Most Great! [O Allah, from You and to You.] O Allah, accept it from me.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #246 · Muslim 3/ 1557, Al-Bayhaqi 9/287.

categories.name: To ward off the plot of the rebellious devils
categories.arabicName: ما يقول لرد كيد مردة الشياطين
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #247
entries.arabic: ((أَعُوذُ بكَلِمَاتِ اللَّهِ التَّامَّاتِ الَّتِي لاَ يُجَاوِزُهُنَّ بَرٌّ وَلاَ فَاجِرٌ: مِنْ شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ، وَبَرَأَ وَذَرَأَ، وَمِنْ شَرِّ مَا يَنْزِلُ مِنَ السَّمَاءِ، وَمِنْ شَرِّ مَا يَعْرُجُ فيهَا، وَمِنْ شَرِّ مَا ذَرَأَ فِي الْأَرْضِ، وَمِنْ شَرِّ مَا يَخْرُجُ مِنْهَا، وَمِنْ شَرِّ فِتَنِ اللَّيْلِ وَالنَّهَارِ، وَمِنْ شَرِّ كُلِّ طَارِقٍ إِلاَّ طَارِقاً يَطْرُقُ بِخَيْرٍ يَا رَحْمَنُ)).
entries.transliteration: Acûdhu bi-kalimâti l-lâhi t-tâmmâti l-lâti lâ yujâwaizuhunna barrun wa lâ fâjirun min sharri mâ khalaqa wa bara'a wa dhara'a, wa min sharri mâ yanzilu mina s-amâci wa min sharri mâ yacrujuhu fîhâ, wa min sharri mâ dhara'a fî-l-ardi wa min sharri mâ yakhruju minhâ, wa min sharri fitani l-layli wa n-nahâri, wa min sharri kullitâriqin illâtâriqan yatruqu bi-khayrin yâ rahmân.
entries.translation: I seek refuge in the Perfect Words of Allah -which neither the upright nor the corrupt may overcome - from the evil of what He created, of what He made, and of what He scattered, from the evil of what descends from the heavens, and of what rises up to them, from the evil of what He scattered in the earth, and of what emerges from it, from the evil trials of night and day, and from the evil of every night visitor, except the night visitor who comes with good. O Merciful One.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #247 · Ahmad 3/419, with an authentic chain of narration, and Ibn As-Sunni (no. 637). Al-Arna'ut, graded its chain authentic in his checking of Al-'Aqidah At-Tahawiyyah p. 133. See also Majma'uz-Zawa'id, 10/127.

categories.name: Seeking forgiveness and repentance
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 6 invocations.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #248
entries.arabic: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((وَاللَّهِ إِنِّي لأَسْتَغفِرُ اللَّهَ وَأَتُوبُ إِلَيْهِ فِي الْيَوْمِ أَكْثَرَ مِنْ سَبْعِينَ مَرَّةٍ)).
entries.transliteration: Not applicable — this entry records Prophetic guidance rather than a separate recited formula.
entries.translation: Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said: "By Allah, I seek the forgiveness of Allah, and repent to Him more than seventy times in a day.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #248 · Al-Bukhari, cf. Al-Asqalani, Fathul-Bari 11/101.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #249
entries.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ تُوبُوا إِلَى اللَّهِ فَإِنِّي أَتُوبُ فِي الْيَوْمِ إِلَيْهِ مِائَةَ مَرَّةٍ)).
entries.transliteration: Not applicable — this entry records Prophetic guidance rather than a separate recited formula.
entries.translation: Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said: "O people, repent to Allah, for I verily repent to Him one hundred times a day.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #249 · Muslim 4/2076.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #250
entries.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((مَنْ قَالَ أَسْتَغْفِرُ اللَّهَ الْعَظيمَ الَّذِي لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ هُوَ الْحَيُّ القَيّوُمُ وَأَتُوبُ إِلَيهِ، غَفَرَ اللَّهُ لَهُ وَإِنْ كَانَ فَرَّ مِنَ الزَّحْفِ)).
entries.transliteration: Astaghfiru l-lâha-l-cazîma l-ladhî lâ ilâha illâ huwa-l-hayyu-l-qayyûmu, wa atûbu ilayhi.
entries.translation: Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said: Whoever says: I seek the forgiveness of Allah the Mighty, Whom there is none worthy of worship except Him, the Living, the Eternal, and I repent to Him, Allah will forgive him even if he has deserted the army's ranks.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #250 · Abu Dawud 2/85, At-Tirmidhi 5/569, and Al-Hakim who declared it authentic and Ath-Thahabi agreed with him 1/511. Al-Albani graded it authentic in Sahih At-Tirmidhi 3/182. See also Jami'ul-'Usool li-'Ahdaith Ar-Rasool 4/ 389-90 checked by Al-Arna'ut.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #251
entries.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((أَقْرَبُ مَا يَكُونُ الرَّبُّ مِنَ الْعَبْدِ فِي جَوْفِ اللَّيْلِ الآخِرِ فَإِنِ اسْتَطَعْتَ أَنْ تَكُونَ مِمَّنْ يَذْكُرُ اللَّهَ فِي تِلْكَ السَّاعَةِ فَكُنْ)).
entries.transliteration: Not applicable — this entry records Prophetic guidance rather than a separate recited formula.
entries.translation: Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said: "The closest that the Lord comes to the slave is in the last portion of the night. So, if you are able to be among those who remember Allah in this hour, then be among them."
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #251 · At-Tirmidhi, An-Nasa'i 1/279 and Al-Hakim. See also Al-Albani, Sahih At-Tirmidhi 3/183, and Jdmi'ul-'Usool with Al-Arna'ut's checking 4/144.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #252
entries.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((أَقْرَبُ مَا يَكُونُ الْعَبْدُ مِنْ رَبِّهِ وَهُوَ سَاجِدٌ فَأَكثِرُوا الدُّعَاءَ)).
entries.transliteration: Not applicable — this entry records Prophetic guidance rather than a separate recited formula.
entries.translation: Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said: "The closest that the slave comes to his Lord is when he is prostrating, so invoke Allah much (in prostration)."
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #252 · Muslim 1/350.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #253
entries.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((إِنَّهُ لَيُغَانُ عَلَى قَلْبِي وَإِنِّي لأَسْتَغْفِرُ اللَّهَ فِي الْيَوْمِ مِائَةَ مَرَّةٍ)).
entries.transliteration: Not applicable — this entry records Prophetic guidance rather than a separate recited formula.
entries.translation: Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said: "It is a heavy thing for my heart if I do not seek Allah's forgiveness a hundred times a day."
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #253 · Muslim 4/2075. Ibn 'Athir explains that the Prophet (ﷺ) was always vigilant in his remembrance and drawing near to Allah, and if he forgot to do any of what he normally did from time to time, or it slipped his mind, he felt as if he had wronged himself and so he would begin to seek the forgiveness of Allah. See Jami'ul-'Usool 4/386.

categories.name: Excellence of remembrance and glorification of Allah
categories.arabicName: فضل التسبيح و التحميد، و التهليل، و التكبير
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 12 invocations.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #254
entries.arabic: قَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم مَنْ قَالَ: سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ وَبِحَمْدِهِ فِي يَوْمٍ مِائَةَ مَرَّةٍ حُطَّتْ خَطَايَاهُ وَلَوْ كَانَتْ مِثْلَ زَبَدِ الْبَحْر)).
entries.transliteration: Subhâna l-lâhi wa bi-hamdihi.
entries.translation: How perfect Allah is and I praise Him.[one hundred times daily]
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #254

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #255
entries.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((مَنْ قَالَ لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لاَ شَرِيكَ لَهُ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ، وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ، وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ عَشْرَ مِرَارٍ، كَانَ كَمَنْ أَعْتَقَ أَرْبَعَةَ أَنْفُسٍ مِنْ وَلَدِ إِسْمَاعِيلَ)).
entries.transliteration: Lâ ilâha illâ l-lâhu, wahdahu lâ sharîka lahu, lahu-l-mulku wa lahu-l-hamdu, wa huwacalâ kulli shay'in qadîr.
entries.translation: None has the right to be worshipped except Allah, alone, without partner. To Him belongs all sovereignty and praise and He is over all things omnipotent. [ten times]
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #255

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #256
entries.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((كَلِمَتَانِ خَفِيفَتَانِ عَلَى اللِّسَانِ، ثَقِيلَتَانِ فِي الْمِيزَانِ، حَبِيبَتَانِ إِلَى الرَّحْمَنِ: سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ وَبِحَمْدِهِ، سُبْحانَ اللَّهِ الْعَظِيمِ)).
entries.transliteration: Subhâna l-lâhi wa bi-hamdihi. Subhâna l-lâhi-l-cazîm.
entries.translation: [Aboo Hurayrah reported that the Messenger of Allah (saws) said : (There are) two words, (which are) light on the tongue, heavy on the scale and beloved to The Most Gracious :]How perfect Allah is and I praise Him. How perfect Allah is, The Supreme.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #256

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #257
entries.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((لَأَنْ أَقُولَ سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ، وَالْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ، وَلاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ، وَاللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، أَحَبُّ إِلَيَّ مِمَّا طَلَعَتْ عَلَيْهِ الشَّمسُ)).
entries.transliteration: Subhâna l-lâhi, wa-l-hamdu li-l-lâhi, wa lâ ilâha illâ l-lâhu, wa l-lâhu akbar.
entries.translation: [Aboo Hurayrah reported that the Messenger of Allah (saws)said : Saying ... :] How perfect Allah is, and all praise is for Allah. None has the right to be worshipped except Allah, and Allah is the greatest. [...is more beloved to me than everything the sun has risen over.]
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #257

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #258
entries.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((أَيَعْجِزُ أَحَدُكُم أَنْ يَكْسِبَ كُلَّ يَوْمٍ أَلْفَ حَسَنَةٍ)) فَسَأَلَهُ سَائِلٌ مِنْ جُلَسَائِهِ كَيْفَ يَكْسِبُ أَحَدُنَا أَلْفَ حَسَنَةٍ؟ قَالَ: ((يُسَبِّحُ مِائَةَ تَسْبِيحَةٍ، فَيُكتَبُ لَهُ أَلْفُ حَسَنَةٍ أَوْ يُحَطُّ عَنْهُ أَلْفُ خَطِيئَةٍ)).
entries.transliteration: Subhanallah.
entries.translation: The Prophet asked whether one could earn a thousand good deeds each day. He explained: say “Glory is to Allah” one hundred times; a thousand good deeds are recorded or a thousand sins are removed.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #258

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #259
entries.arabic: ((مَنْ قَالَ: سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ الْعَظِيمِ وَبِحَمْدِهِ غُرِسَتْ لَهُ نَخْلَةٌ فِي الْجَنَّةِ)).
entries.transliteration: Subhâna l-lâhi-l-cazîmi wa bi-hamdihi.
entries.translation: [Jabbir related that the Prophet (saws) said : Whoever says :] How perfect Allah is, The Supreme, and I praise Him. [...a palm tree is planted for him in paradise.]
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #259

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #260
entries.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((يَا عَبْدَ اللَّهِ بْنَ قَيْسٍ أَلاَ أَدُلُّكَ عَلَى كَنْزٍ مِنْ كُنُوزِ الْجَنَّةِ))؟ فَقُلْتُ: بَلَى يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، قَالَ: ((قُلْ لاَ حَوْلَ وَلاَ قُوَّةَ إِلاَّ بِاللَّهِ)).
entries.transliteration: Lâhawla wa lâ quwwata illâ bi-l-lâhi.
entries.translation: There is no might nor power except with Allah.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #260

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #261
entries.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((أَحَبُّ الْكَلاَمِ إِلَى اللَّهِ أَرْبَعٌ: سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ، وَالْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ، وَلاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ، وَاللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، لاَ يَضُرُّكَ بِأَيِّهِنَّ بَدَأتَ)).
entries.transliteration: Subhâna l-lâhi, wa-l-hamdu li-l-lâhi, wa lâ ilâha illâ l-lâhu, wa l-lâhu akbar.
entries.translation: How perfect Allah is, and all praise is for Allah. None has the right to be worshipped except Allah, and Allah is the greatest.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #261

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #262
entries.arabic: جَاءَ أَعْرَابِيٌّ إِلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صلى الله عليه وسلم فَقَالَ: عَلِّمْنِي كَلاماً أَقُولُهُ: قَالَ: ((قُلْ: لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لاَ شَرِيكَ لَهُ، اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ كَبِيراً، وَالْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ كَثِيراً، سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ رَبِّ العَالَمِينَ، لاَ حَوْلَ وَلاَ قُوَّةَ إِلاَّ بِاللَّهِ الْعَزِيزِ الْحَكِيمِ)) قَالَ: فَهَؤُلاَءِ لِرَبِّي، فَمَا لِي؟ قَالَ: ((قُلْ: اللَّهُمَّ اغْفِرْ لِي، وَارْحَمْنِي، وَاهْدِنِي، وَارْزُقْنِي)).
entries.transliteration: Lâ ilâha illâ l-lâhu, wahdahu lâ sharîka lahu. Allâhu akbaru kabîran, wa-l-hamdu li-l-lâhi kathîran. Subhâna l-lâhi rabbi-l-câlamîna. Lâhawla wa la quwwata illâ bi-l-lâhi-l-cazîzi-l-hakîm.Allâhumma ghfir lî, wa rhamnî, wa hdinî, wa rzuqnî.
entries.translation: None has the right to be worshipped except Allah, alone without partener. Allah is most great and much praise is for Allah. How perfect Allah is, Lord of the worlds. There is no might nor power except with Allah, The Exalted in might, The Wise. O Allah, forgive me, have mercy upon me, guide me and grant me sustenance.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #262

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #263
entries.arabic: كَانَ الرَّجُلُ إِذَا أَسْلَمَ عَلَّمَهُ النَّبيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم الصَّلاَةَ ثُمَّ أَمَرَهُ أَنْ يَدْعُوَ بِهَؤُلاَءِ الْكَلِمَاتِ: ((اللَّهُمَّ اغْفِرِ لِي، وَارْحَمْنِي، وَاهْدِنِي، وَعَافِنِي وَارْزُقْنِي)).
entries.transliteration: Allâhumma ghfir lî, wa rhamnî, wa hdinî, wacâfinî, wa rzuqnî.
entries.translation: O Allah, forgive me, have mercy upon me, guide me, give me health and grant me sustenance.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #263

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #264
entries.arabic: ((إِنَّ أَفْضَلَ الدُّعَاءِ الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ، وَأَفْضَلَ الذِّكْرِ لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ)).
entries.transliteration: Alhamdu li-l-lâh.Lâ ilâha illâ l-lâh.
entries.translation: All praise is for Allah. None has the right to be worshipped except Allah.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #264

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #265
entries.arabic: الباقيات الصالحات : سبحان الله والحمد لله ، ولا إله إلا الله ،والله أكبر ،و لا حول ولا قوة إلا بالله
entries.transliteration: Subhâna l-lâhi, wa-l-hamdu li-l-lâhi, wa lâ ilâha illâ l-lâhu, wa l-lâhu akbaru, wa lâhawla wa lâ quwwata illâ bi-l-lâhi.
entries.translation: How perfect Allah is, and all praise is for Allah. None has the right to be worshipped except Allah, and Allah is the greatest. There is no might nor power except with Allah.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #265

categories.name: How the Prophet (ﷺ) ﷺ made tasbeeh
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #266
entries.arabic: عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو رضي الله عنه قَالَ: ((رَأَيْتُ النَّبيَّ صلى الله عليه وسلم يَعْقِدُ التَّسْبِيحَ)) وفي زيادةٍ: ((بِيَمِينِهِ)).
entries.transliteration: Not applicable — this entry records Prophetic guidance rather than a separate recited formula.
entries.translation: Abdullah bin 'Amr (RA) said: "I saw the Prophet (ﷺ) counting the glorification of his Lord on his right hand."
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #266 · Abu Dawud with a different wording 2/81, and At-Tirmidhi 5/521. See also Al-Albani, Sahihul-Jami'As-Saghir 4/271 (no. 4865).

categories.name: Comprehensive types of good and manners
categories.description: Complete Hisnul Muslim collection with 1 invocation.

entries.title: Hisnul Muslim #267
entries.arabic: قَالَ النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم ((إِذَا كَانَ جُنْحُ اللَّيْلِ – أَوْ أَمْسَيْتُم – فَـكُفُّوا صِبْيانَـكُم، فَإِنَّ الشَّيَاطِينَ تَنْتَشِرُ حِينَئِذٍ، فَإِذَا ذَهَبَ سَاعَةٌ مِنَ اللَّيلِ فَخَلُّوهُمْ، وَأَغْلِقُوا الأَبْوَابَ وَاذْكُرُوا اسْمَ اللَّهِ؛ فَإِنَّ الشَّيطَانَ لاَ يَفْتَحُ بَاباً مُغلَقاً، وَأَوْكُوا قِرَبَكُمْ، وَاذْكُرُوا اسْمَ اللَّهِ، وَخَمِّرُوا آنِيَتَكُم، وَاذْكُرُوا اسْمَ اللَّهِ، وَلَوْ أَنْ تَعْرُضُوا عَلَيْهَا شَيْئاً، وَأَطْفِئُوا مَصَابِيحَكُمْ)).
entries.transliteration: Not applicable — this entry records Prophetic guidance rather than a separate recited formula.
entries.translation: When night falls, restrain your children (from going out) because at such time the devils spread about. After a period of time has passed, let them be. Shut your doors and mention Allah’s name, for verily the devil does not open a shut door, tie up your water-skins and mention Allah’s name, cover your vessels with anything and mention Allah’s name and put out your lamps.
entries.source: Hisnul Muslim #267 · Al-Bukhari with Al-Fath 10/88 and Muslim 3/1595.
